title: OpenClaw Expert
description: Job Title: OpenClaw Expert Needed: MS Teams Bot Integration with Gemini API Job Description: We are looking for a developer experienced with the OpenClaw framework to help us finalize and debug a local bot deployment. We have already done the foundational setup on a Mac environment, but we are hitting a wall with the final integration points between OpenClaw , Microsoft Azure Bot Services, and the Google Gemini API. We need an expert to jump in, audit our configuration, fix the connectivity issues, and get the bot communicating smoothly in Microsoft Teams. Current Architecture & Stack: Framework: OpenClaw (v2026.x) running locally via the CLI ( openclaw gateway). Channel Plugin: @ openclaw /msteams configured using the channels JSON block. LLM Backend: Google Gemini (google/gemini-1.5-pro-latest). Tunneling: Ngrok forwarding local port 18789 to Azure. Platform: Microsoft Teams (via Azure Bot / MS Teams Developer Portal). OS: macOS (Terminal / Zsh). Specific Issues to Solve: API Rate Limiting / Authentication: The bot successfully receives messages from Teams via ngrok, but immediately throws an "API rate limit reached. Please try again later" error. We need help properly injecting our enterprise Gemini API key into the OpenClaw environment to bypass fallback limits. Configuration Audit: Review our openclaw .json and hidden credential files to ensure the App ID, App Password, and API keys are properly mapped. Azure / Teams Alignment: Verify that our Azure Bot endpoint and MS Teams Developer portal settings are correctly synchronized with the OpenClaw gateway. Requirements: Proven experience deploying and configuring OpenClaw bots. Strong understanding of Azure Bot Service and the Microsoft Bot Framework. Experience with Google AI Studio / Google Cloud API key management. Comfortable debugging Node.js/NPM dependency issues and macOS terminal environments. To Apply: Please briefly mention your experience with OpenClaw and state how you typically handle injecting API credentials into local OpenClaw gateway deployments.

title: Openclaw Custom Workflow setup
description: I got this workload to be done 1. Agent Design: Modular, Hand-off Oriented Outreach Agent: - Uses PhantomBuster for automated LinkedIn prospecting and messaging. - Gathers responses and identifies positive/negative/neutral engagements. - Hands off enriched lead data (with all available LinkedIn context) to the next agent. Enrichment Agent: - Validates and supplements profile/company details (using available public data). - Qualifies leads based on criteria (title, company size, engagement level). - Prepares data for CRM entry and flags high-priority leads. CRM/Scheduling Agent: - Equipped with GoHighLevel API access under your subaccount. - Creates/updates lead records. - Schedules appointments directly on Vin’s calendar. - Sends intro emails or draft responses (saved, not sent) for Some one's review. Reporting/Follow-Up Agent: - Tracks the delivery stage for competitive analysis. - Coordinates reminders and ensures the final consult is booked. 2. Additional Strategic Recommendations LinkedIn Data Limits: - PhantomBuster has usage quotas and LinkedIn can throttle/break some automations. Design agents to handle rate limits gracefully and escalate when reached. Audit Logging: - Every agent interaction should be logged for compliance and campaign improvement. Winston, as COO, retains oversight. Lead Scoring: - Agents should score leads based on responses, title, and activity to triage effort and personalize follow-ups. Personalization Library: - Maintain templates (with variables) for direct vs soft-touch messaging, editable by your team, so agents aren’t static. Guardrail Enforcement: - Implement business rules in the agent code to prevent mis-targeting (e.g. always verify title/company matches before message is sent). Human Override: - At any escalation point (high-value or unclear engagement), agents can queue a review to human.

title: OpenClaw AI Specialist
description: OpenClaw AI Specialist — Buyer/Seller Matching System for Medical Imaging Equipment Ongoing Hourly | Segmented Projects | Long-Term Relationship ABOUT US Amber Diagnostics (AmberUSA.com) is one of the most established medical imaging equipment dealers in the United States — 30+ years buying, selling, renting, and refurbishing CT scanners, MRI machines, PET/CT systems, and mobile imaging trailers. We operate nationally and maintain an active database of buyers, sellers, and available inventory. We are now building out a foundational AI infrastructure starting with OpenClaw — to intelligently match potential equipment buyers with available inventory sourced from our CRM, email, and key external websites. PROJECT OVERVIEW We need an experienced OpenClaw specialist to design and deploy Phase 1 of our AI-powered buyer/seller matching system, running on a dedicated Mac Mini. This is not a research role — we need someone who has built real, production-ready OpenClaw systems and can move fast. This engagement is structured as an ongoing hourly relationship, billed per segmented project phases, with the intent to continuously expand our AI infrastructure over time. The right person becomes our go-to AI automation partner. PHASE 1 SCOPE OF WORK 1. SALESFORCE CRM INTEGRATION - Connect OpenClaw to Salesforce via REST API / OAuth 2.0 - Pull and normalize Contact, Lead, and Opportunity data - Extract buyer criteria: modality, budget, geography, status - Extract seller data: equipment type, model, asking price, condition - Map custom fields and sync on a defined schedule 2. EXTERNAL WEBSITE SCRAPING - Build scrapers for DotMed.com, eBay Medical, and 2-3 additional equipment listing platforms - Extract: equipment specs, pricing, seller contact info, listing date - Normalize scraped data against internal buyer criteria - Schedule automated scrape cycles (daily or on-demand) 3. BUYER/SELLER MATCH REPORTS - Build automated data sheets and match reports identifying buyer-to-inventory pairings - Output: Google Sheets, Excel, or structured CSV with match score, equipment details, and contact info - Reports should be clean, readable, and ready to act on — not raw data dumps - Flag high-confidence matches separately for immediate follow-up 4. AUTOMATED EMAIL RESPONSES - Trigger personalized outbound emails when a strong match is identified - Integrate with SendGrid (our current email platform) or Gmail - Build email templates for: buyer match notification, seller equipment inquiry, and general follow-up sequences - Include logic to avoid duplicate outreach to the same contact 5. MAC MINI DEPLOYMENT AND INFRASTRUCTURE - Install and configure OpenClaw on a dedicated Mac Mini (hardware provided) - Set up as a 24/7 background service with auto-restart - Implement basic monitoring and error logging - Document all configurations for internal reference REQUIRED SKILLS - Hands-on OpenClaw experience (real deployments, not theory) - Salesforce API — REST, SOQL, OAuth, Connected Apps - Web scraping — Playwright, Puppeteer, Selenium, or equivalent - Python and/or Node.js / TypeScript - Email automation — SendGrid, Gmail API, or equivalent - Data normalization and structured output (Google Sheets / Excel / CSV) - macOS deployment and background service configuration - REST APIs, Webhooks, and API key management - Clear written and verbal communication — you will work directly with the owner BONUS SKILLS (nice to have, not required) - LLM / Claude / OpenAI API integration for enhanced match scoring - Prompt engineering for intelligent classification of buyer intent - n8n, Make.com, or Zapier for workflow orchestration - RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) for AI-assisted matching logic - Vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate) for semantic search across listings - MCP Server configuration - Experience with medical equipment, DotMed, or B2B equipment marketplaces PHASE 1 DELIVERABLES 1. Fully configured OpenClaw instance running 24/7 on Mac Mini 2. Live Salesforce integration — buyer and seller data actively pulling 3. Minimum 3 active web scrapers (DotMed plus 2 external sources) 4. Automated match report output (Google Sheet or Excel, updated on schedule) 5. Automated email workflow — match alerts firing via SendGrid or Gmail 6. Full documentation and screen-share walkthrough upon completion ENGAGEMENT STRUCTURE - Billing: Hourly, per segmented project phase - Phase 1 estimated scope: 20-40 hours - Ongoing: Phases 2, 3, and beyond will follow immediately for candidates who perform well - Location: US-based strongly preferred (data security — Salesforce CRM access involved) - Communication: Direct with business owner — no account managers, no middlemen - Timeline: Looking to begin within the week TO APPLY — PLEASE INCLUDE: - 1-2 examples of OpenClaw systems you have built and deployed (describe the use case) - Your approach to the Salesforce API connection and buyer/seller data extraction - How you would structure the matching logic between buyer criteria and scraped listings - Your hourly rate and estimated hours for Phase 1 - Your availability to start We are a results-driven company with 30 years in the industry. We know what we want to build, we have the data, and we are ready to invest in the right person for a long-term partnership. If you genuinely understand OpenClaw at a production level — we want to hear from you.

title: OpenClaw Audit - Claude Code Expert Needed
description: I'm a non-technical founder who vibe codes everything with Claude Code. Every product I've shipped — trading bots, dashboards, websites, automation pipelines — was built entirely through Claude Code/ Openclaw on a dedicated Mac Mini M4 running 24/7. No traditional coding background. Just me talking to Claude and shipping. I now have a complex setup — 24 repos, 14 plugins, custom rules/hooks/skills, automated deploy pipelines, trading bots, and a multi-layer memory system. It works, but it has friction. I need a Claude Code power user to audit my configuration and fix the issues I can't see from the inside. This is NOT a "set up Claude Code for me" job. I've built multiple revenue-generating products through vibe coding. I need someone who understands Claude Code at an infrastructure level to optimize what I've already built. How I Work: I vibe code everything. I describe what I want, Claude builds it, I iterate. No IDE, no manual coding. This is how I built: **Automated trading bots** — live data pipelines, custom dashboards (Next.js + Supabase), PM2 process management **AI sales automation** — outbound outreach workflows using N8n and other tools **Web development** — 10+ websites for my businesses and clients, all built through Claude Code **Graphic design** — need to systematize Instagram content creation (current bottleneck) **Agent infrastructure** — custom identity system, global + per-project CLAUDE.md files, multi-layer deploy pipeline, automated memory Everything ships same-day. I don't do sprints or phases. The setup needs to match that speed. What I Need Audited & Fixed 1. Behavioral Routing (Priority: HIGH) Claude sometimes takes the wrong approach. Example: I ask about dashboard data and it opens Playwright to screenshot the page instead of querying the database or reading the code. My CLAUDE.md has tool routing rules but they're not consistently followed. I need someone to diagnose WHY and restructure the instructions so Claude picks the right tool every time. 2. Memory System (Priority: HIGH) Memory doesn't carry between sessions reliably. Claude forgets project context mid-conversation. I have a multi-layer memory system (CORRECTIONS.md, LEARNINGS.md, PATTERNS.md, auto-memory) but I'm not sure it's structured optimally. Need an audit and redesign if warranted. 3. Project Structure (Priority: MEDIUM) 24 git repos, only 14 have CLAUDE.md files Project Registry is stale, duplicate directories exist Need: CLAUDE.md for every active project, cleanup plan for dead/duplicate repos, standardized folder structure 4. Rules, Hooks & Plugins (Priority: MEDIUM) 5 global rules files — potential conflicts and redundancies Hookify guardrails defined in global CLAUDE.md but not enforced in individual project .claude/ dirs 14 plugins installed — need to verify all are useful and none conflict Custom skills that may need consolidation 5. Instagram Design Pipeline (Priority: MEDIUM) No systematized workflow for generating on-brand social graphics Need a repeatable Claude Code skill/workflow: brand guidelines in, polished IG graphics out Should leverage existing tools (Gemini imagegen, frontend-design skill, MCP servers) 1. **Audit document** — every issue found, severity-ranked, with file paths and specific fixes 2. **Updated configuration** — global CLAUDE.md, per-project CLAUDE.md files, rules, settings.json, hookify rules, soul.md etc. 3. **Project cleanup plan** — what to archive, rename, merge 4. **Memory system improvements** — better structure for cross-session context 5. **Design pipeline** — working skill for Instagram graphics 6. **Verification** — before/after proof that the fixes actually change Claude's behavior Requirements Deep Claude Code experience (the CLI tool, not just Claude chat). You should know what CLAUDE.md, rules files, hooks, MCP servers, plugins, and skills are without Googling them. Understand the vibe coding workflow — I'm not a developer, I'm a builder. The setup needs to make Claude smarter for a non-technical operator, not add complexity that requires engineering knowledge to maintain. Comfortable with macOS, TypeScript/Next.js, Python, Bash, Supabase, PM2 Has optimized Claude Code for other users or yourself at a similar complexity level Available for 1-2 follow-up check-ins after the initial audit My Stack Mac Mini M4, macOS, always-on Claude Code Opus 4.6, high effort, agent teams enabled. I'm currently shuffling 7 Claude Pro Max Accounts TypeScript/Next.js (most projects), Python (trading bots), Bash (infra) Vercel, Supabase, PM2, GitHub

description: We are running OpenClaw locally within a dedicated WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) environment on a sandboxed laptop. Our Current Stack: A single main agent utilizing 11 expert persona skills. Additional core skills: self-improvement, agent-browser (snapshot-based), and summarize. Local Whisper AI integrated for capturing Telegram voice notes, transcribing, and executing them flawlessly. The Core Challenges & Deliverables: 1. WSL Browser Relay & Token Optimization: We cannot run the native Chrome browser-relay extension due to WSL limitations. We currently use agent-browser to take snapshots (e.g., logging into Zapier), but this consumes way too many API tokens. We need a highly token-efficient browser solution or a fixed headless WSL setup so the AI can browse, read, and act autonomously without burning our budget. 2. Dynamic Model Routing (Sub-agents): We need automatic and smart model switching. The system should route simple tasks and conversational heartbeats to cheap models (like GPT-4.1-mini) and autonomously spawn premium models (like GPT-5.1 or Claude) only for complex reasoning and heavy lifting. 3. Absolute Autonomy & SaaS Credit Consumption: We have over 30 different Lifetime Deal (LTD) SaaS tools with monthly regenerating credits. The AI must: Analyze our business needs and strategically plan how to use these tools. Autonomously EXECUTE and CONSUME these credits to perform actual work (generating SEO content, running outreach, automating posts, On-page/Off-page SEO, backlink distribution) without waiting for human prompts. Fully manage our emails, social media accounts, and website health independently. 4. Continuous Learning & Self-Improvement: The AI must have a persistent memory. It needs to remember important context, analyze its own mistakes, and continuously improve its workflows. It must proactively provide daily updates on: what was completed, what is currently in progress, and what is queued next. 5. Custom Mission Control Dashboard: Existing dashboards like LobsterBoard and TenacitOS do not meet our needs. We need a custom, clean UI that provides: Clear visual boards (To-Do, Doing, Done). A direct chat interface to communicate with OpenClaw . File management and task queuing. An emergency "Kill Switch" and an interface for high-level human approvals when necessary. A "Self-Improvement Log" to see exactly what the AI learned and optimized today. Application Requirement: If you are a true OpenClaw wizard, start your proposal by telling me exactly how you plan to solve the WSL browser limitation and the token-heavy snapshot issue. OpenClaw System Review Review configuration files Validate gateway configuration Review Docker container setup Confirm environment variables and API key configuration Verify NGINX reverse proxy configuration ⸻ 2. Skills Framework Setup Install and configure a core set of OpenClaw skills. Tasks include: • Ensuring skills are properly registered and callable by the model • Validating skill execution behavior • Establishing a repeatable process for adding or updating skills • Documenting the skill installation workflow ⸻ 3. Responsibilities include: • Reviewing available skills with the owner • Determining required APIs, credentials, and third-party services for each skill • Coordinating access requirements with the owner • Assisting with configuration of required API keys and integrations • Enabling and validating each skill within the OpenClaw gateway The goal is to ensure the platform has a well-defined and operational set of skills available to the AI agent. ⸻ 4. Messaging Channel Setup Configure and test messaging channels including: • WhatsApp integration • Webhooks • Message routing Future channel expansion may include: • iMessage • additional communication channels ⸻ 5. Memory System Stabilization Diagnose why memory persistence is failing. Tasks include: • Reviewing memory configuration • Validating storage backend • Testing recall and write operations • Ensuring long-term memory works reliably ⸻ 6. Security Hardening Review security posture of the deployment. Tasks include: • Review gateway exposure • Secure environment variables and API keys • Verify reverse proxy configuration • Recommend operational safeguards ⸻ 7. Documentation Provide documentation covering: • Adding new skills • Managing messaging channels • Updating configuration safely • Best practices for maintaining the OpenClaw gateway ⸻ Required Skills Candidates should have experience with: • OpenClaw or comparable AI agent frameworks • Docker deployments • Linux / VPS administration • NGINX reverse proxy • Webhooks and API integrations • OpenAI API integration ⸻ Bonus Experience • WhatsApp Business API integrations • AI agent orchestration frameworks • LLM tool / function calling architectures • Secure API key management ⸻ Deliverables 1. Fully operational OpenClaw gateway 2. WhatsApp messaging functioning end-to-end 3. Skills framework configured and usable 4. Memory persistence functioning correctly 5. Documentation for managing and expanding the system ⸻ Access Access may be provided via: • Temporary VPS access • Relevant configuration files API keys will not be shared directly unless required and may be added by the owner where appropriate. ⸻ Engagement Structure Project-based engagement with milestone payments. Additional work may be available if the engagement is successful. ⸻ Screening Questions Please include the following in your proposal: 1. Have you worked with OpenClaw specifically? 2. If not, which AI agent frameworks have you worked with? 3. Describe a Docker-based AI system you deployed. 4. What approach would you take to securely deploy and manage AI skills that require external API access? Milestone 1 — Environment Review & Architecture Assessment Goal: Confirm the OpenClaw deployment is architecturally sound before making changes. Deliverables: • Review of VPS, Docker containers, and OpenClaw configuration • Validation of NGINX reverse proxy and gateway exposure • Identification of misconfigurations or missing components • Written remediation plan for the remaining milestones Outcome: A documented assessment of the current deployment and a clear plan to stabilize the environment. ⸻ Milestone 2 — Core Platform Stabilization Goal: Ensure the OpenClaw gateway and underlying environment are stable. Deliverables: • Correct environment variables and container configuration • Confirm OpenAI model connectivity • Validate system health via OpenClaw diagnostic tools • Confirm gateway stability and reliable operation Outcome: OpenClaw gateway running reliably without configuration errors. ⸻ Milestone 3 — Skills Framework Deployment Goal: Implement a reliable and repeatable skills system. Deliverables: • Identify required skills with project owner • Install and configure selected skills • Configure API integrations required for each skill • Ensure skills are callable by the LLM • Demonstrate successful skill execution Outcome: OpenClaw can dynamically call installed skills. ⸻ Milestone 4 — Messaging Channel Integration Goal: Enable messaging communication through OpenClaw . Deliverables: • Configure WhatsApp integration • Verify webhook connectivity • Confirm inbound and outbound messaging • Test message routing through the OpenClaw gateway Outcome: Messages sent through WhatsApp successfully reach the OpenClaw agent and responses are returned. ⸻ Milestone 5 — Memory System Stabilization Goal: Ensure persistent memory functions correctly. Deliverables: • Diagnose and resolve memory persistence issues • Confirm storage backend configuration • Test memory write and recall operations Outcome: Long-term memory operates reliably. ⸻ Milestone 6 — Security Review & Documentation Goal: Ensure the system can be safely maintained and expanded. Deliverables: • Security review of deployment • Recommendations for credential management • Documentation covering: • Skill installation • Channel configuration • Environment updates • Operational best practices Outcome: Owner can safely maintain and expand the OpenClaw system. ⸻ Technical Screening Checklist Use these questions when reviewing candidates or during an interview. The goal is to quickly determine if they actually understand OpenClaw -like systems. ⸻ 1. OpenClaw Architecture Knowledge Ask: “Explain the role of these OpenClaw components.” • Gateway • Skills • Memory • Channels • Agents A qualified candidate should explain how these components interact within the system. Red flag: They describe OpenClaw like a simple chatbot. ⸻ 2. Docker Infrastructure Experience Ask: “How would you troubleshoot a Docker container that is running but not responding through a reverse proxy?” Expected topics: • container logs • port exposure • reverse proxy configuration • network bridging Red flag: Only suggests restarting the container. ⸻ 3. Skill Invocation Architecture Ask: “How do LLM-based agents decide when to call tools or skills?” Expected concepts: • tool descriptions • model reasoning • structured outputs / function calling • prompt structure Red flag: They claim skills are triggered manually only. ⸻ 4. Webhook Troubleshooting Ask: “What steps would you take if a messaging webhook is not triggering the OpenClaw gateway?” Expected topics: • endpoint reachability • TLS certificates • reverse proxy routing • provider logs Red flag: No mention of webhook verification or routing. ⸻ 5. Secure API Key Handling Ask: “How do you safely manage API keys for AI tools running in Docker?” Expected answers: • environment variables • secret managers • avoiding hard-coded credentials Red flag: They suggest storing keys directly in code. ⸻ Ideal Candidate Profile Strong candidates will typically have experience with: • AI agent frameworks • Docker infrastructure • LLM tool/function calling systems • API integrations • messaging webhooks Many qualified candidates may have worked with: • LangChain • AutoGPT • OpenClaw -like agent frameworks • AI automation platforms

description: I am looking for an experienced AI / automation developer to design and implement a complete solution that will take me from zero to a working AI agent that can handle routine tasks in the browser‑based game Total Battle. www.totalbattle.com This will run on a virtual private server (VPS), not on my personal PC, so that my own computer remains free for normal work. Project Goal Create a reliable, configurable AI agent that can log into Total Battle in a web browser on a VPS and perform predefined in‑game tasks automatically, using some combination of: Claude (for decision‑making and strategy logic) OpenClaw or a similar agent/automation framework (for driving the browser and orchestrating tools) Additional tools/skills you recommend (browser automation, scheduling, logging, etc.) The result should be a usable setup with documentation, not just a proof‑of‑concept script. Important Notes This is a technical automation and AI orchestration project: I am explicitly looking for someone who understands both LLM “brains” and browser automation “hands.” The agent must run entirely on a VPS environment (e.g., Linux‑based server) that I can manage, with clear deployment steps. I am open to OpenClaw hosts. I would also like Zoom/GoogleMeet screen sharing discussions so that I understand how all of this works. High‑Level Requirements System Design and Setup Help choose an appropriate VPS configuration and OS for this use case. Install and configure all required components (runtime, dependencies, game browser environment, etc.). Set up the AI/agent stack: Claude integration, OpenClaw (or similar), and any supporting services. Browser Automation for Total Battle Automate login and session management in a dedicated browser profile. Implement robust browser control for Total Battle: Navigating UI screens and menus Clicking buttons and links Reading key info from the page (DOM / text / possibly screenshots + OCR) Design the automation with resilience to minor UI changes and pop‑ups. AI Agent Logic (Claude + Skills) Treat Claude (or equivalent model) as the “brain,” receiving state/context and returning structured actions. Implement a loop where the agent: Captures current game state from the browser Decides what to do based on simple strategies/policies I provide Calls automation tools to execute those actions Make it configurable via high‑level rules, for example: "Fulfill Crypting Requirements" "Find Merc Exchanges" "Maximize Epic Troop Calculations" Scheduling and Background Operation Run fully on the VPS, ideally without needing a visible desktop session once configured. Support scheduled or continuous operation, with reasonable limits to mimic human‑like behavior (delays, randomization, etc.). Provide a simple way for me to start, stop, and monitor the agent. Monitoring, Logging, and Safety Implement basic logging of: Actions taken by the agent Errors or unexpected UI states Provide a straightforward way for me to: Review logs Adjust parameters (timers, priorities, etc.) Include basic fail‑safes so the agent stops or pauses if something looks clearly wrong (e.g., unexpected screens). Hand‑Off and Documentation Clear, step‑by‑step setup documentation so I can: Re‑deploy on another VPS if needed Update API keys/config values Short guide on: How to start/stop the system How to adjust simple behavior rules How to troubleshoot common issues Ideal Freelancer Skills Strong experience with LLM‑based agents (Claude, OpenAI, similar) and tool‑calling / function‑calling patterns. Hands‑on experience with browser automation, such as Playwright, Puppeteer, Selenium, or a similar framework. Familiarity with agent frameworks like OpenClaw or comparable systems, and how to plug in custom skills/tools. Comfortable deploying and maintaining systems on Linux VPS environments. Experience automating web games or complex web apps is a plus. Ability to communicate tradeoffs clearly (safety, ToS risk, performance, cost). What to Include in Your Proposal A brief description of your relevant experience, especially: AI agents / LLM tool‑use projects Browser automation projects Any game or MMO‑related automation work (if applicable) A short outline of how you would architect the solution (stack, tools, and rough phases). An estimate of: Timeline (with milestones) Budget or hourly rate If you have examples of similar automation/agent builds (screenshots, code samples, or short descriptions), please share them. I am ready to move quickly once I find someone who clearly understands both the AI and automation sides of this project.

title: OpenClaw + Claude Setup (Mac Mini AI Assistant Sandbox)
description: Build “Ace” – Local AI Founder Assistant ( OpenClaw + Claude + Mac Mini) Job Description I am looking for a technical freelancer to help set up a lightweight personal AI assistant called “Ace” on my Mac Mini. Ace will act as my Founder Operating Assistant. The goal is to create a simple system that helps me: • track decisions • track commitments • capture ideas • organise projects • generate daily founder briefings This project is not a business intelligence system and should not analyse ecommerce data. A separate AI system is being built to analyse business performance. Ace is strictly a personal operating assistant. System Overview Ace will run locally on a Mac Mini and use: OpenClaw as the assistant runtime Claude API as the reasoning engine Markdown files (.md) as the assistant’s memory The system should be simple, modular, and easy to maintain. Scope of Work Phase 1 – OpenClaw Setup Install and configure OpenClaw locally on macOS. Tasks: • install dependencies • configure runtime • ensure OpenClaw runs locally • verify prompts work correctly Deliverable: A working OpenClaw assistant running locally on the Mac Mini. Phase 2 – Claude Integration Connect the system to Claude via API. Tasks: • configure API connection • test prompts • verify assistant responses Deliverable: Assistant able to communicate with Claude successfully. Phase 3 – Memory System Setup Create a simple file-based memory system using Markdown files. Folder structure example: AceOS/ memory/ SOUL.md MEMORY.md PROJECTS.md DECISIONS.md TASKS.md IDEAS.md DAILY.md PEOPLE.md Tasks: • create templates for each file • configure OpenClaw so the assistant reads these files • confirm assistant can reference stored information Deliverable: Assistant successfully reading and updating memory files. Phase 4 – Founder Assistant Workflows Implement simple workflows including: Daily Founder Brief Generate a summary including: • top priorities • open loops • outstanding commitments • suggested focus for the day Decision Logging Example input: Decision: Stop ordering Metcon 9 after July Assistant logs decision in: DECISIONS.md Commitment Tracking Example input: Reminder: Follow up Corey tomorrow Assistant logs commitment in: TASKS.md Idea Capture Example input: Idea: TWL footwear bundle with socks Assistant saves idea to: IDEAS.md Phase 5 – Messaging Interface Connect the assistant to: Telegram This will allow me to: • send messages to Ace from my phone • log decisions and tasks • request daily briefings Deliverable: Working Telegram bot connected to the assistant. Security and Data Access You will have access to sensitive business data including client records, CRM systems and communications. Being located in Australia is not required, although some time zone overlap with Australia is preferred. We are open to hourly, contract or long-term arrangements for the right candidate. //Application Requirements To apply, please include a short description of AI systems or automation frameworks you have built, any GitHub or portfolio links if available, and a Loom video explaining a system you have designed. The appointment scheduling should be a little more straight forward as it takes place outside of facebook. The final setup should run reliably on end users device or via a virtual machine and allow end user or inhouse to manage and monitor everything easily. Project Objectives OpenClaw or other automations Setup & Configuration The main task is to install and configure OpenClaw or other automations and ensure it runs correctly on end users machine or via virtual machine This includes: • Installing and configuring the OpenClaw environment • Setting up OpenClaw agents and task workflows • Ensuring OpenClaw can run scheduled automation tasks • Creating a stable setup that runs safely on my system • Documenting the OpenClaw setup so it can be maintained Experience specifically working with OpenClaw automation agents is strongly preferred. 1. Vehicle Listing Automation OpenClaw or whatever other automation you suggest, should automate the workflow for creating Facebook Marketplace vehicle listings. Required functionality: • Retrieve vehicle photos, descriptions and specifications from a dealership inventory page • Navigate to Facebook Marketplace listing creation page • Upload vehicle photos • Populate listing fields such as: Make Model Mileage Colour Price Description Operational rules: • Maximum 10 listings per day • Listings should be deleted and reposted after 7 days to refresh visibility • Automation should operate with human-like timing and behaviour Important context: I already own and operate a Chrome extension that performs this listing workflow with a few clicks. The automation could either: A) Trigger and use the extension or B) Replicate the process independently, removing the need for the extension Whichever approach is more stable and maintainable. C) can only post their dealerships inventory to Facebook Marketplace via their personal profile, not via business page so not able to set up official api's etc, so posting to marketplace is currently getting done via my chrome browser extension which is done via users interface. 2. Messenger Lead Handling OpenClaw or whatever other automation you suggest, should assist with handling Facebook Marketplace Messenger enquiries. Requirements: • Detect new incoming messages • Respond using trained conversation prompts • Handle common buyer questions • Maintain natural conversation flow Lead engagement logic: • If a customer stops responding, the system should follow up for up to 5 days • The objective of the conversation is to secure a viewing appointment Important context For automating chat reply's on a personal facebook account with in marketplace messenger, example cant use manychat or official api's etc, we need some form of desktop app or browser extension installed on end users device etc. there is browsers available on chrome store that we could use on end users device but unsure how good they are. i also made a desktop app last year to try achieve this but it still needs some work to polish. 3. Appointment Scheduling & Notifications Once a viewing appointment is agreed, the system should: • Record the appointment • Send notification to the salesperson via email or WhatsApp Optional integration: • Google Calendar or similar scheduling tool Customer reminders should be sent: • Morning of appointment • 1 hour before appointment Possible channels: • Messenger • SMS • WhatsApp • Email 4. Command Interface (WhatsApp Control) The system should support simple command inputs from the user via WhatsApp or similar messaging interface. Examples: • Prioritise posting specific vehicles • Reschedule appointments • Adjust daily tasks This allows the salesperson to control or modify the automation remotely. Technical Expectations The solution should: • Run reliably on a user’s machine or controlled environment via virtual machine • Require minimal technical knowledge from the user • Be stable enough to potentially deploy to multiple dealership users In an ideal world i would like to provide a fully "done for you or hands off" experience for the end user where we handle/automate everything in house but as we can not log into their accounts from different locations constantly i have 2 options. set up everything above on a Virtual machine (log into their fb via same static IP address each time), or install everything on end users device (their IP address) and let then use/monitor themselves (downside here is they might break it or interrupt it and ring for support often) The solution should: • Not compromise account security • Be stable and maintainable • Include clear documentation If successful, this automation could be rolled out to car dealerships and sales teams using my existing network and software platform. i would sell it, you would install it on end users device and maintain it. When applying please include: • Your experience with OpenClaw • Examples of automation systems or AI workflows you have built • Your approach to building stable automation setups

AI Agent Architecture A modular agent framework including: • Lead Intake Agent • Email Communication Agent • Scheduling Agent • CRM Update Agent • Proposal Assistant Agent • Vendor / RFP Agent • Integrator Monitoring Agent Agents should communicate through structured workflows rather than operating independently. 2. title: Daily AI Driven Operations Business Accountant + OpenClaw | Full-Time
description: AI Finance Systems & Operations Lead Read This Before Applying This is not a traditional bookkeeping role. This is not an end-of-month reporting role. This is not a role for someone who only wants to process transactions and stay inside one software platform. This role is for someone who understands AI systems, workflow design, automation logic, and operational accounting, and can work directly with our accountant / Financial Controller to build a finance operations machine that works across multiple business units in real time. You do not need to be the final decision-maker on accounting treatment. That sits with the accountant / Financial Controller. You do need to understand enough about Xero, entity separation, payroll flow, BAS/GST workflow, accounts payable, accounts receivable, reconciliations, and financial controls to help design systems that are clean, usable, scalable, and safe. Your job is to help us build the AI-enabled accounting operations layer for the group so that the right tasks are surfaced, the right data moves to the right place, the right people are involved, and nothing gets missed. If you are strong in AI tools, automation thinking, systems design, structured operations, and can work intelligently with finance people without overstepping accounting authority, keep reading. About Vision Group Vision Group (VIGS) is an AI-enabled holding company operating across trade and professional services in Australia. Our business units include roofing, remedial construction, concreting, electrical, plumbing, project management, and a digital marketing agency. Each business unit has its own operating requirements. Each entity has its own Xero file. We run a lean offshore team through Monday.com, OpenClaw , structured SOPs, and clear workflows. Our Financial Controller oversees the financial integrity of the group. This role exists to help build the AI and systems layer around accounting operations, so the group can scale without relying on messy handovers, scattered information, or founders being dragged into daily financial workflow issues. The Role You are the AI Finance Systems & Operations Lead for the Vision Group. Your role is to work with the accountant / Financial Controller and operations leadership to design, improve, and maintain an AI-supported accounting operations system across multiple entities. You will help turn recurring accounting tasks into a structured operating environment using OpenClaw , Monday.com, Xero workflows, SOPs, automations, dashboards, exception handling, and escalation pathways. You are not here just to “do bookkeeping.” You are here to help us build the machine that makes multi-entity accounting operations cleaner, faster, safer, and easier to scale. That means: Mapping finance workflows properly Identifying repetitive tasks that can be structured or automated Building task logic and accountability into Monday.com Working with the accountant to ensure the accounting side is correct Working with operations to ensure the workflows actually get followed Helping create AI-supported guardrails, prompts, checks, dashboards, and exceptions handling Making sure AI assists the team without ever making financial decisions You should be comfortable moving between: finance workflow thinking AI / automation thinking process mapping SOP creation operational problem-solving cross-functional communication with finance and ops What This Role Looks Like in Practice You will work around the existing accounting operations environment, which includes recurring tasks such as: daily A/P and A/R tracking across business units invoice entry workflows across ServiceM8 and Xero project cost allocation checks payroll workflow support BAS / GST workflow preparation Amex reconciliation processes entity separation controls software cost reviews month-end reporting workflow preparation access and systems control management recurring Monday.com finance board management daily and monthly financial sign-off workflow design Your role is to help build and improve the system that makes all of that work properly. That includes: reviewing the current finance task structure identifying what should be manual, what should be assisted by AI, and what should be automated improving task flow between staff, accountant, FC, and leadership building or refining dashboards, board logic, automations, and escalation paths helping document the operating rules so the workflow survives staff changes making sure each business unit can run inside a clear structure rather than relying on memory Core Objective Build an AI-enabled finance operations system that supports real-time accounting workflows across multiple entities without allowing AI to make financial decisions. The outcome we want is simple: The accountant stays in control of accounting judgement. The system handles structure, visibility, reminders, routing, checks, and workflow control. The business gets real-time clarity. The founders are not the bottleneck. What You Will Be Responsible For 1. Finance Workflow Design Map current accounting and finance operations across all business units Identify workflow gaps, bottlenecks, duplicated effort, and unclear ownership Help redesign recurring finance workflows so they are operationally tight and scalable Ensure every recurring finance process has a clear owner, due date, status, and escalation path 2. AI-Supported Operations Design Work with OpenClaw and related AI tools to structure finance support workflows Build AI-assisted systems for: overdue task surfacing missing data flags anomaly prompts recurring reminders internal summaries board resets exception tracking SOP surfacing Help define where AI adds value and where it must stop 3. Monday.com Finance Board Architecture Build, refine, and maintain the finance operations board in Monday.com Ensure recurring accounting tasks are structured correctly across daily, weekly, fortnightly, and monthly cycles Create clean status logic, owner logic, and escalation logic Ensure the board reflects reality at all times 4. Accounting Workflow Support Understand how the accounting process works in practice across: Xero payroll BAS/GST workflows reconciliations project/entity allocation supplier invoice capture accounts payable / receivable Work with the accountant / Financial Controller to ensure your systems support correct accounting treatment Translate accounting requirements into operational workflows the team can actually follow 5. SOPs, Rules, and Guardrails Build or refine SOPs for accounting operations workflows Create operational rules for how tasks move through the system Ensure AI guardrails are documented and followed Help build repeatable workflows so new team members can slot into the system without chaos 6. Cross-Functional Coordination Liaise with the Financial Controller, accountant, COO, operations staff, and relevant support team members Ensure finance workflows are usable by the people involved, not just theoretically correct Surface system issues early before they create reporting, payroll, compliance, or cashflow problems AI Guardrails Are Non-Negotiable This role works closely with AI. That does not mean AI controls financial decisions. What AI can do Surface overdue items and missing steps Flag anomalies, inconsistencies, and possible entity errors Assist with workflow routing and task reminders Draft internal notes, summaries, and process documentation Help structure data for human review Support board resets, recurring task generation, and SOP lookup Help identify repetitive work that can be systemised What AI cannot do AI does not approve payments AI does not initiate payments AI does not execute payroll payments AI does not decide coding treatment AI does not decide cost allocation between entities AI does not access banking or payment authority AI does not hold supplier passwords, bank credentials, or sensitive financial access AI does not replace accountant judgement All financial decisions remain human-led. The accountant / Financial Controller owns accounting judgement. You own the system design and workflow quality around that judgement. Tools You Will Work In Monday.com – finance operations boards, workflows, status logic, accountability OpenClaw – AI orchestration, prompts, support logic, workflow assistance Xero – understanding required for workflow design across multiple entities ServiceM8 – invoice and job-linked operational flow understanding Microsoft Teams / WhatsApp – communication and task follow-up Amex systems – understanding daily monitoring and receipt workflows Other automation / AI tools where relevant and approved What We Are Looking For Strong understanding of AI tools, automation logic, systems design, and workflow architecture Good operational understanding of accounting processes, especially: Xero multi-entity workflow payroll workflow BAS/GST process flow A/P and A/R structure reconciliations project and entity separation Comfortable working directly with an accountant / Financial Controller without pretending to be the accountant Able to translate finance requirements into structured systems and operating processes Strong experience building systems in Monday.com or similar workflow tools Comfortable creating SOPs, rules, logic, dashboards, prompts, and escalation pathways High attention to detail and respect for financial controls Strong written English and structured communication Comfortable working in an AI-enabled environment with strict guardrails Available 11am to 7pm Sydney time, Monday to Friday What We Are Not Looking For Traditional bookkeepers who only want data entry work End-of-month accountants who do not think operationally Pure automation people with no respect for accounting controls Anyone who thinks AI should make financial decisions Anyone who needs constant direction to know what to work on Anyone who cannot work across systems, people, and process design Anyone who gets messy around entity separation or workflow accountability How Success Is Measured A clear finance operations system is built and maintained across the group Monday.com reflects the real finance workflow accurately at all times Repetitive accounting operations are structured, documented, and improved OpenClaw is integrated into finance workflows in a controlled and useful way The accountant / Financial Controller has stronger visibility, fewer manual follow-ups, and cleaner inputs Finance tasks move through the business with less founder involvement Entity separation and workflow accountability are stronger, not weaker SOPs, dashboards, and operating logic are documented and usable The system becomes more scalable as more business units are added Who You Will Work With You will work closely with: Financial Controller / accountant – for accounting logic, treatment, controls, compliance COO – for workflow integration, blockers, operational implementation CEO / founders – for strategic alignment when needed Operations team – for task execution and workflow reality AI / systems layer – to build useful support systems without crossing guardrails Problem-Solving Hierarchy This role follows a structured hierarchy: Step 1: OpenClaw first Use OpenClaw to identify the problem, locate SOPs, review prior patterns, and generate a proposed path forward. Step 2: Solve through the system Work through the issue yourself using the board, SOPs, workflow logic, and available tools. Step 3: Escalate to COO / Financial Controller Escalate only after you have identified the issue clearly, reviewed the system, and formed a proposed solution. Step 4: Escalate higher only when necessary Founders should not be dragged into workflow problems that should have been solved lower down. Engagement Rate: AUD $3.00 per hour Hours: Full-time, 38 hours per week Working hours: 11am to 7pm Sydney time, Monday to Friday Location: Remote Start: Immediate Reports to: COO, working closely with Financial Controller / accountant Structure: Long-term role with growth as the group scales How to Apply Send a short note, not a template, answering all four questions below: Describe your experience with AI tools, automations, or systems building in an operations environment. What did you build, improve, or automate? What is your understanding of accounting operations across Xero, payroll, reconciliations, BAS/GST, A/P, and A/R? Where have you worked with these processes directly? Give an example of a workflow, reporting, or operational problem you helped solve by combining systems thinking with process discipline. What was broken and what did you change? How would you work with an accountant or Financial Controller to build an AI-supported accounting operations system without overstepping financial decision-making authority? Applications that do not answer all four will not be reviewed.

We value your time, so you will be compensated the full $250 at the end of the 1-week period regardless of how much you complete. However, candidates who successfully complete this test project to our standards within the timeframe will unlock the opportunity to take on significantly more ongoing, high-paying subcontractor work with us. **Project Overview** You have exactly 7 days to build and deploy a functional multi-agent AI assistant using the OpenClaw framework, accompanied by a user-friendly Web Admin Portal. This test evaluates your understanding of agentic workflows, multi-channel I/O, vision models, UI development, system security, and your ability to ship working software. **High-Level Scope & Deliverables** * **Web Admin Portal:** Build a user-friendly dashboard (any tech stack) for non-technical users to view chat histories, track API usage, and toggle OpenClaw skills. * **Multi-Channel Communication:** Integrate real-time instant replies via Telegram and an email parsing/threading solution that processes and replies in under 15 minutes. * **Dynamic LLM Routing & Memory:** Implement persistent long-term memory and utilize OpenRouter to default to cost-effective models (e.g., Gemini 2.5 Flash) for simple tasks, escalating to robust models (e.g., Claude 3.5 Sonnet/Opus) for complex reasoning. * **Security Governance:** Engineer an interception layer that automatically vets and prevents malicious skills (e.g., unauthorized OS commands) from being executed. * **Specialized Sub-Agents:** * *CRM Agent:* Uses Go High Level (GHL) API to update contact records. * *Bookkeeping Agent:* Uses vision capabilities to extract data from receipt images, ask clarifying questions, and append categorized data to a Google Sheet via API. * *Custom Agent:* Propose one additional useful sub-agent designed for small business owners. **Key Requirements & Next Steps** * **Strict 1-Week Timeline:** The final solution (in whatever state it is in) must be deployed to a live cloud environment and handed over exactly 7 days after the contract start date. * **Live Handoff Call:** At the end of the 7 days, you must attend a scheduled 30-60 minute live handoff call to walk through your code, explain your architecture, and discuss challenges. * **READ THE ATTACHED DOCUMENT:** This description is just a summary. **Before applying, you must read the attached complete specification document.** It contains the exact technical requirements, the APIs we provide, environment setup instructions, and the specific User Stories / Test Cases you will be evaluated against. description: About the opportunity We are a home furnishings manufacturing business building a technology platform on top of our manufacturing foundation. We need one engineer to build the complete backend infrastructure from the ground up. The technical specification is complete — every workflow, every AI agent, every database, and every integration is documented in detail. Your job is to read the spec, scope the work, and build it. This is a serious, multi-tool AI and automation stack. If you have shipped production-grade work on n8n, Claude API, and Notion before, you will know within 30 minutes of reading the spec whether this is the right project for you. There is potential for ongoing work as the platform scales. We are hiring for the build scope first. The stack n8n Cloud · Claude API · OpenClaw · Notion · Clay · Apify · Google Workspace APIs · Slack · Typeform · Instantly · DocuSign · Various third-party APIs What the work involves Building and connecting a set of AI agents, automation workflows, databases, and signal intelligence tools that work together as a single platform operating system. The scope includes: Multi-modal AI agents that process images and produce structured outputs written to a Notion data layer Scheduled and event-driven automation pipelines including a multi-step delivery pipeline with defined SLA monitoring A Notion-based data layer with multiple interconnected databases, a calculated scoring tool, and a document generation flow via Google Docs API Web scraping via Apify actors for multiple data sources on scheduled and event-triggered runs A real-time signal intelligence configuration in Clay across multiple source types and a composite scoring model A research data pipeline connecting multiple third-party APIs to a central intelligence store Five role-specific AI assistant instances in Slack connected to Claude via OpenClaw , each trained on structured output formats and hard operational boundaries Everything is specified. You build to the spec, test against defined acceptance criteria, document what you have built, and hand over cleanly to the internal team. What we need from you n8n — production-level experience building complex multi-step workflows. Webhooks, scheduled triggers, conditional routing, API calls, error handling, and retry logic. Real builds that teams depend on. Claude API — experience building structured AI agents with prompt engineering for consistent, parseable outputs. Multi-modal input handling including base64-encoded images. OpenClaw — hands-on experience configuring OpenClaw or equivalent AI assistant platforms (Dust, Lindy, custom Slack-Claude integrations). Connecting instances to downstream tools and setting hard operational boundaries. Apify — Playwright-based actor builds. You understand scrapers break and build with maintainability in mind. Notion API — Notion as a data layer for automation. Databases, relations, formula fields, and API reads and writes — not Notion as a note-taking tool. Python — comfortable writing Python scripts inside an n8n Execute Command node. API integrations — Google Docs API, Typeform webhooks, Slack API, Google Drive API, Clay, DocuSign. Comfortable with API documentation and building without pre-built connectors. Discipline — builds to a spec, tests against defined criteria, documents clearly, and hands over in a way the internal team can maintain. Availability — this is a focused project that needs proper time. Not looking for someone fitting it in between five other engagements. Nice to have Prior experience on a similar stack — n8n plus Claude API plus Notion as the data layer. Familiarity with Clay's native integrations and HTTP request node approach. Experience chaining structured AI agent outputs into downstream automation.

title: Automation Expert | Make.com, API Integrations, OpenPhone & N8N (Hourly – Long-Term)

title: OpenClaw Multi-Agent System on Mac mini (WhatsApp + Google Tools + Home Assistant)
description: I need a senior freelancer to set up OpenClaw locally on a Mac mini and deliver a production-ready multi-agent assistant. Must include multi-provider fallback routing via OpenRouter (OpenAI + Claude + Gemini), WhsApp-based channel separation, integrations with Gm.ail / Chrome / Google Drive / Google Calendar / Todoist / Notion / macOS Terminal, plus Home Assistant smart home control via text. Core Requirements (Must-have) 1. OpenClaw Install + Reliability Follow official docs, resolve dependencies launchd auto-start after reboot + restart strategy Logs + simple run/restart/update commands 2. Multi-Provider + Fallback via OpenRouter All provider routing goes through a single OpenRouter API key — no direct OpenAI / Claude / Gemini keys Fallback chain (A→B→C) with rate-limit / timeout / retry / error recovery handled at OpenRouter level Model assignments and routing rules defined in a dedicated, human-readable config file (e.g. openrouter_config.yaml) so the owner can update models, swap providers, or adjust cost limits without touching agent code Easy to change routing/models post-delivery 3. W..sApp Channel Separation One bot account + 5 separate W..sApp groups: Chef, Finance, Health, Home, Business Each group contains only me + bot All messages go to Chef first (router/orchestrator) Final response posted back to the originating group (topic isolation) 4. Agents Chef: routing + prioritization + balanced model, escalates for complex requests Financial: portfolio tracking, file commentary, relevant updates, planning brainstorm Health: workout / meal / supplement / sleep / skincare tracking + weekly summary; research Q&A Home: errands + planner + smart home control Business: brainstorm → break down → Todoist tasks 5. Integrations (all must-have) Todoist: all agents can create tasks; labels will be provided and integrated per workflow Notion: full access inside a dedicated agent-only workspace Gmail: no sending (phase 1). Process label atlas → summarize + ask what to do → create Todoist tasks + create Google Calendar events if date/time is clear + draft reply text (no send) Chrome: automation allowed Google Drive: read/search + upload/create allowed; no delete/move/permission changes macOS Terminal: allowed with confirmations for destructive actions Google Calendar: auto-create events when date/time is clear; ask if ambiguous 6. Smart Home via Home Assistant Home Assistant (local IP, same LAN) is source of truth Text commands trigger scenes + device on/off + simple delays Risky actions (locks / alarms / cameras) require W..sApp confirmation 7. Security Secrets stored in Keychain / encrypted config Least-privilege + clear auditability API key management centralized via OpenRouter 8. Backup System backed up via Time Machine; agent configs and Notion data must be documented for manual restore Live Acceptance Test (AnyDesk) Must demonstrate live: W..sApp routing in all 5 groups → correct agent selection → response in same group Todoist task creation by multiple agents Gmail label triage → summary + Todoist task + Calendar event Finance: send a file → summary + portfolio commentary + tasks Health: workout/meal log → guidance + tracking; plus one research Q&A Home: smart home command triggers Home Assistant; risky action asks confirmation Runbook + short walkthrough Milestone for payment release: Chef agent + at least 1 sub-agent fully operational and passing acceptance test. Post-delivery support: 15 days bug fix included. To Apply Relevant projects ( OpenClaw / local assistants / W..sApp routing / macOS automation / Home Assistant) Proposed approach + milestones + price (hourly or fixed) Demo video / screenshots are a plus

title: AI Architect / Systems Engineer for Custom AI Business Operating System
description: Upwork Job Post AI Systems Architect / Full Stack Builder for Modular Business Operating System ( OpenClaw + Workspace + Agent Architecture) Overview I’m looking for a highly capable AI systems architect / full stack builder to help design and implement a modular AI operating system for my businesses. This is not a simple chatbot project. I am building a dialogue driven, approval based AI business operating system that can organize knowledge, support decision making, assist with research and outreach, manage workflows, and help operate multiple creative businesses through separate workspaces and specialized agents. The system should be modular, practical, and built for real operational use. The first phase is to create the bare bones infrastructure inside OpenClaw , then build a structure where I can create multiple workspaces, and inside each workspace have dedicated agents / modules that operate on the knowledge and workflows of that workspace. This is a long term build, but I want to start with a strong systems thinker who can help architect it correctly from the beginning. What I’m Building I currently have two primary business ecosystems that this AI operating system needs to support. 1. Resonance This currently includes: Resonance Art Collective A curated platform / network for visionary, mission driven projects, artists, collaborators, and large scale interdisciplinary ideas involving art, architecture, ecology, technology, and social impact Resonance Design Studio A separate design and consulting studio focused on immersive architecture, bamboo construction, sustainable materials, resorts, restaurants, community spaces, prefabricated bamboo systems, and future product / marketplace development Marketplace layer Artist designed and studio designed goods such as lighting, objects, jewelry, furniture, and design products, with an emphasis on thoughtful production rather than generic handmade marketplace items 2. Roots Alchemy A culinary brand / studio focused on: private chef work retreats culinary experiences proposals and outreach content and marketing recipe and operations systems What the System Needs to Do The AI operating system should become the central intelligence layer across these workspaces. It should help with: knowledge management file indexing and retrieval project understanding research and opportunity discovery lead tracking and CRM support outreach drafting proposal generation document generation content planning task and priority support budget and cost tracking workflow support across different businesses This system must be human guided. It should assist, organize, and draft, but not act externally without approval. Core Design Goals These are the most important design goals for the system. 1. Human Guided, Approval Based Operation The system must support decisions and recommend actions, but all external actions require review and approval. Examples: do not send outreach automatically do not change live content automatically do not contact leads without approval do not publish, message, or commit externally without approval 2. Persistent Memory and Organized Knowledge The system must use my Google Drive and structured business files as the source of truth and maintain persistent understanding across workspaces. It should be able to retrieve, summarize, and use prior documents, proposals, renderings, budgets, recipes, outreach language, etc. 3. Modular, Low Maintenance, High Quality Architecture I do not want a fragile automation maze. The system should be: modular cleanly organized easy to maintain scalable across businesses built for quality rather than speed alone I want infrastructure that can grow over time without becoming messy. Desired Outcomes The ideal result is a system where I can: open a workspace view relevant knowledge, documents, leads, opportunities, and tasks interact with agents specialized for that workspace retrieve approved language and files quickly generate drafts, plans, and research based on indexed source materials maintain separation between workspaces while using shared infrastructure underneath Existing Documentation and Inputs I already have a substantial amount of strategic documentation, summaries, and organized content. Current materials include things like: master strategy documents workspace summaries brand voice and communications docs website and platform planning project summaries budgets and financial frameworks outreach language social media strategy portfolio planning ecosystem structure documents design / construction summaries AI workflow ideas approval logic and workflow direction Primary source of truth is Google Drive. Important source material types include: Google Docs / Docs exported to Word / Markdown PDFs pitch decks spreadsheets renderings budgets proposals website copy images video recipes and menus CRM data project files System Structure I’m Envisioning Phase 1 Bare Bones Infrastructure in OpenClaw This phase should create the basic system architecture. Needs include: core system structure workspace logic ability to add workspaces ability to attach knowledge sources to workspaces base UI / dashboard or basic interface layer initial memory / retrieval structure initial agent structure Phase 2 Workspace Setup Initial workspaces include: Resonance Roots Alchemy Each workspace should have its own: knowledge base approved language CRM structure opportunity tracking relevant files and documents goals and workflows Phase 3 Agent / Module Layer Agents should be specialized by function, not just general chat. Examples of desired modules / agents: Research and Opportunity Agent Lead Discovery and Qualification Agent Personalized Outreach Drafting Agent Content and Communications Agent Proposal and Document Agent Operations and Priority Agent Budget / Cost Tracking Agent Schedule and Timeline Agent File Organization and Knowledge Agent Culinary Operations Agent for Roots Alchemy Phase 4 Controlled Integrations and Workflow Expansion Examples: stronger Google Drive integration website content support CRM improvements reporting layers controlled browser based tasks controlled outbound workflow support Technical Direction I am open to discussion, but current direction likely includes a hybrid architecture: Local / controlled environment for: file access browser automation scripts desktop workflows Cloud model layer for: reasoning summarization generation decision support Likely or possible technologies: OpenClaw Python Next.js Playwright Vercel Google Drive integration vector retrieval / RAG modular agent orchestration frameworks OpenAI or similar APIs I do not need blind loyalty to one stack. I need someone who can make good architectural decisions. What I Need From You I need someone who can help with both architecture and implementation. That includes: helping refine the overall system design structuring workspaces and agents correctly proposing a clean technical architecture building the first usable version thinking long term, not just hacking together automations balancing usability with scalability I do not need someone who only knows prompt chaining. I need someone who understands how to build practical AI enabled internal systems. Ideal Experience Strong fit if you have experience with: AI agent systems orchestration frameworks knowledge retrieval systems file and document indexing modular dashboards / internal tools LLM based workflows approval based automations CRM style workflow systems browser automation production ready full stack implementation Bonus if you have worked on: operating systems for small businesses AI workflow tools creative or design driven businesses multi workspace systems internal knowledge platforms Important Constraints Please understand these clearly. This system must remain human approval based Quality matters more than speed I do not want a bloated or overengineered system I do not want uncontrolled agents acting externally The build should support long term expansion across multiple ventures Google Drive is the current source of truth Separate workspaces are required Shared infrastructure underneath is preferred What Success Looks Like A successful first phase would give me: a clean system architecture a working base inside OpenClaw the ability to create separate workspaces at least one or two functional agents/modules inside those workspaces a clear knowledge retrieval layer using my existing documents a roadmap for future phases

title: Senior Prompt / AI Systems Engineer
description: # Senior AI Systems / Prompt Engineer Remote · Format negotiable: full-time / part-time / project-based ## About Us We are a fast-growing logistics company with a strong in-house IT and AI infrastructure. We already have our own CRM, AI agents, automated calculations, document processing, notifications, SLA management, and partial automation of internal workflows. AI is not an experiment or an "extra feature" for us — it is a real operational layer of the business that already impacts request processing speed, communication quality, internal coordination, and team efficiency. We are now at the point where we need to move beyond writing individual prompts and instead systematically build, stabilize, and scale the entire AI logic of the company. ## Why We're Hiring for This Role We already have several agents in production, CRM integrations, structured outputs, routing logic, and automation across client-facing and internal workflows. The challenge is that the system has grown complex: much of it relies on manual oversight, ad hoc agreements, and lives in one person's head. Any change to one agent can affect an adjacent process, and some of the logic is not yet sufficiently formalized, documented, or testable. We need someone who will take ownership of the AI function: understand the current system, bring order to what exists, improve stability, eliminate gray areas, and help scale everything further — without chaos. ## What We Already Have - Custom-built CRM - Client-facing AI agents - Scenarios for qualification, cost estimation, FAQ, summaries, checklists, and SLA - Structured JSON responses and data-passing logic between agents and the system - Automation of some internal and client-facing processes - AI logic embedded in real business tasks, not in a test environment - Active exploration of OpenClaw as a potential new branch of AI infrastructure development For us, AI is part of the overall chain: **User → Agent → Data collection & processing → CRM / internal logic → Team → Next step** That's why it's not just about the quality of model responses — it's about the reliability of the entire system: no data loss, no broken logic, and agents that behave predictably. ## What You'll Be Doing ### Initially - Study the current agents, their roles, and real-world usage scenarios - Analyze existing prompts, logic, and weak points - Identify duplication, chaos, instability, and hidden dependencies - Structure the architecture of the AI logic layer - Eliminate gray areas and unpredictable behavior - Bring order to prompt versions, scenarios, and change tracking - Build baseline test cases for key scenarios ### Ongoing - Develop and refine agents for new business requirements - Improve accuracy, stability, and predictability of responses - Reduce hallucinations, unwanted improvisation, and failures at process handoffs - Maintain JSON logic and data flow into the CRM - Investigate real errors and edge cases reported by the team - Collect user feedback and turn it into systematic improvements - Contribute to new AI initiatives, including OpenClaw - Gradually help transform the AI layer from a collection of solutions into a clear, maintainable system ### Dedicated Focus Area - Build a testing ground for agents where conversations can be simulated and bulk-tested before deploying changes to production - Create an environment for verifying agent behavior across standard, complex, and edge-case scenarios - Develop automated prompt improvement pipelines based on accumulated data - Identify contradictions, overlaps, and recurring patterns in the behavior of clients, managers, and agents - Work with large volumes of already-collected conversations and scenarios to improve system behavior - Participate in building HITL (human-in-the-loop) mechanics for agent fine-tuning and adjustment - Move the AI layer from being manually configurable to gradually self-improving — under human oversight and with clear rules for making changes ## Who We're Looking For We don't need someone who simply "writes prompts." We need someone who can build AI logic as a system and understand how it operates within a business. ### Required - Experience building system prompts for real business use cases - Understanding of how LLMs behave in practice: limitations, hallucinations, instruction-following, context management, few-shot techniques, etc. - Experience with structured outputs, JSON, and scenarios where model responses feed into downstream systems - Systems thinking and the ability to see the whole process, not just a single chat response - Independence and willingness to navigate an already-running, live, and imperfect system - Ability to distinguish whether a problem lies in the prompt, the data, the process, or the architecture itself ### Nice to Have - Basic Python skills - Understanding of APIs and integration logic - Experience with Claude / OpenAI / other LLM APIs - Experience with evals, AI testing, regression checks - Experience with knowledge bases / RAG - Experience in AI architecture, automation, or CRM workflows - Experience building test environments, conversation simulators, or agent playgrounds - Experience working with large conversation datasets, behavioral analysis, and HITL approaches - Interest in OpenClaw or experience with similar approaches to AI context and memory management ### What Matters in a Person - Quickly grasps context - Takes ownership - Thinks in systems, not fragments - Can independently identify problems and propose solutions - Understands how AI impacts processes downstream - Can work not just with prompts, but with data, patterns, and overall system behavior quality - Brings order and makes systems more reliable — not just "smarter" ## What We Offer - Fully remote work - Flexible engagement format - Direct access to decision-makers - A live system where much has already been built and is ready to be strengthened - Significant influence over the development of the entire AI layer - Opportunity to grow alongside the system and contribute to new initiatives - Compensation discussed individually

Project style: I prefer to start with a clear plan and build a strong core system first, then expand into multiple workflows after the foundation is working. title: AI Assistant Setup Specialist - Linux Server Management
description: I run a service that sets up personal AI assistants for business owners. Each customer gets their own AI bot running 24/7 on Telegram that handles email, drafts documents, and manages tasks. THE BUSINESS: • We charge customers $300-500 for setup + optional $50-100/month support • Customer pays their own AI usage (~$50/month) and server costs (~$15/month) • We handle all the technical setup and maintenance • Growing business with real demand from busy entrepreneurs YOUR ROLE IN THE TEAM: You'll work directly with me (the owner) and a sales person. Here's how it works: • Sales person finds customers and closes deals • They send you customer details: name, business, what they want the bot to do • You set up their bot (3-4 hours per customer) • You maintain all customer bots ongoing • Customer never talks to you directly - sales person handles all communication WHAT YOU'LL DO DAILY: • Set up new customer bots following our documented process • Monitor all customer servers (dashboard with alerts) • Fix issues when something breaks (server restart, config problem, etc.) • Apply updates and optimize bots monthly • Scale: 1-5 customers = easy, 10-20 customers = full-time potential TECHNICAL SETUP PROCESS (what you'll master): 1. Provision VPS server (Hostinger/Hetzner, Debian) 2. Install OpenClaw software (npm install -g openclaw ) 3. Connect to customer's Telegram bot 4. Set up their AI account (Anthropic API) using their credit card 5. Configure bot personality based on their business 6. Connect tools (email monitoring, calendar, web search) 7. Test everything works perfectly 8. Hand over to sales person for customer demo REQUIRED SKILLS: • Linux server administration (SSH, command line, file editing) • Experience with VPS hosting (Hostinger, DigitalOcean, Hetzner, etc.) • Basic Node.js knowledge (npm install, config files) • Comfortable reading documentation and following setup guides • English communication (daily coordination with sales person) • Available for urgent fixes (server down, bot not responding) NICE TO HAVE: • Telegram bot setup experience • Experience with AI APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) • Basic automation/scripting skills • Customer service mindset PAY & WORK: • $5-15/hour, only pay for actual hours worked • Part-time, flexible hours (5-20 hours/week to start) • Remote work, communicate via Telegram group chat • Growth potential: as we get more customers, you get more hours • Potential to become team lead if we hire junior techs PAID TEST TASK (I cover all costs): Instead of you spending money on a VPS, I have a real test customer ready: • Her name is Lana, she's agreed to be our test case • I'll provide you with a VPS and all account details • You set up her AI assistant following our documentation • Timeline: 3-5 days, budget $50-100 for your time • If the setup works perfectly, you're hired for ongoing work ABOUT THE OWNER: I already use an AI assistant myself - it manages my 900 rental properties, handles emails, drafts contracts, coordinates with my team. It genuinely works and saves me hours daily. That's why I'm confident selling this to other business owners. THE OPPORTUNITY: This isn't just a gig - it's a chance to be employee #1 in a growing service business. As we scale from 5 customers to 50+ customers, you'll grow with us. Potential to become technical lead, train other techs, maybe even get equity long-term. TO APPLY: 1. Confirm you have Linux VPS experience (which providers have you used?) 2. Have you worked with Node.js applications before? Any examples? 3. Any experience with APIs, chatbots, or automation? 4. What's your hourly rate within the $10-15 range? 5. Can you start the test task within 3-5 days? 6. Are you looking for ongoing work (not just one-off projects)? Looking for someone reliable who wants to grow with this business long-term. If you're tired of small freelance projects and want to build something bigger, this could be perfect.
