title: Senior AI Engineer — Agentic Systems, Full-Stack (Claude / LLM / TypeScript)
description: Job Description: I'm the founder of a multi-company portfolio spanning energy efficiency, software, direct sourcing, EV infrastructure, and AR inspection technology. I'm building a suite of 17+ proprietary AI-powered tools to run and scale my operations — and I need a senior engineer who can be a true long-term technical partner across this entire ecosystem. This is not a one-off project. This is a foundational engagement with significant runway. The right person grows with the portfolio. The Ecosystem: BC1 / BetterCity One All tools are built under a unified platform ecosystem called BC1 (BetterCity One) — shared infrastructure, auth, and data layers across my companies. This includes BetterCity Energy (30-year NYC energy efficiency firm, 3,000+ projects), Solvur Labs (software & design), Trovi Direct Sourcing, EV Surge, and PROVA (AR inspection tech). The 17+ tool pipeline includes: Ava — AI executive assistant platform (first build, immediate start) BC1 CRM — Full client relationship management (Next.js / PostgreSQL / Prisma / Vercel, existing codebase) BC1 Lighting Catalogue — Product database web app (111 products, indoor/outdoor, Playwright test suite) BC1 Invest — EV charging financial calculator (21-phase model, deployed) SpeakSharp — Communication coaching platform (Read.ai transcript analysis) Sales intake & pipeline tooling for LinkedIn/cold outreach operations ClickUp automation layer (n8n workflows, custom rules engine) PROVA Site Intelligence — AR-adjacent AI inspection tools for the built environment EV charging proposal generator Operational reporting dashboards Additional CRM, marketing, and sourcing tools across portfolio companies This is a living pipeline. New tools are scoped and added regularly. Project 1: Ava / DCC System (Immediate Start) Ava is a personal AI executive assistant built on a two-component architecture: DCC (web platform) — The interface I interact with daily. Built with TypeScript / Fastify / Prisma / PostgreSQL / Redis / BullMQ / React. DCC owns all OAuth tokens and exposes internal API endpoints. OpenClaw (agent layer) — The AI agent runtime. Accesses all external APIs only through DCC endpoints — never directly. A complete documentation suite is already written and ready for handoff: CLAUDE.md, SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TASK.md, and a full DCC API spec. The 16-week build plan is locked with MVP at Week 10. 35 behavioral sections, 18 subsystems. Key integrations: Microsoft Graph, Gmail, ClickUp, Twilio, ElevenLabs, YouTube, Voyage AI, Google Maps, Anthropic API (Claude Haiku for classification, Sonnet for generation) Testing protocol (three-layer): Claude Code → logic validation | OpenClaw / Puppeteer → browser automation | Dan → visual/UX sign-off What I'm Looking For: Must-haves: 5+ years software engineering, 2+ years in production AI/LLM systems Deep hands-on experience with the Anthropic Claude API — tool use, multi-turn conversations, agentic loops Strong TypeScript / Node.js backend (Fastify or Express) PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM Redis + BullMQ task queuing OAuth 2.0 integration (Microsoft Graph and Gmail API required) React frontend — functional and clean (not design-heavy) Git discipline: versioned commits, clean PRs, documented handoffs Comfortable working from detailed architecture docs, SOPs, and spec-driven development Clear async communicator — Loom updates, written status logs, no chasing required Strong bonus: Experience with Claude Code CLI Playwright / Puppeteer automated testing n8n workflow automation ElevenLabs or voice pipeline integration ClickUp API Next.js / Vercel deployment Experience auditing and inheriting existing codebases Working Style & Expectations: I run a documentation-first operation. Every system has a spec. Every build has a task log. You will be expected to update docs as you build — not as an afterthought. You'll work closely with me (the founder/CEO) and coordinate with a Colombia-based team (systems, ClickUp, QA) and a Systems Architect. You are the senior technical lead — you own architecture decisions, code quality, and build sequencing across the ecosystem. I've terminated a prior agency relationship due to poor execution. Type: Hourly, long-term contract Hours: 20–40 hrs/week to start, scaling based on pipeline Communication: Weekly syncs + async Loom/Slack Timezone: Remote, any timezone — EST overlap preferred Documentation: All work accompanied by updated specs and task logs To Apply, Please Include: A specific example of an agentic AI system you've built — not a chatbot, an autonomous agent that takes real-world actions Your direct experience with the Anthropic Claude API (tool use, agents, multi-turn) Links to relevant repos or live projects Your hourly rate One sentence on how you handle inheriting a messy or incomplete codebase Applications without these five items will not be reviewed.

title: AI Bot Building
description: In the past three weeks, we have heard of OpenClaw & different AI l alternatives, and so far this has been a game changer for us. We have been able to create lots of bots that help us within our business and our operations, and we came to simply asking ourselves, "Is there anybody out there that has already mastered the creation of these AI bots?" And that is why we are here. We are looking for the most developed AI enthusiasts out there. We want somebody who is able to look into every single position within our company and automate as many pieces of the puzzle as possible. We believe that AI is an immaculate option because so far it has worked like magic, but we are open to suggestions. Again, our goal is to simply automate the company and replace humans with a lot of bots interacting together. Please understand that it is not our first rodeo down into the AI path. We are already working with AI development teams. It's been over two years now. What we are looking for is truly a specialized person that has the ability to truly revolutionize our company. Please understand that we have had thousands of terrible experiences from people that are over-promising and under-delivering. This happens basically every day with us. And for the person that we choose to work long term with on this role, we will want this person to have a significant amount of experience and proof of concept in order to be hired. We're looking for somebody to work with us long term and have a large budget to pour into this AI takeover development project. We want a true partner. We do not want this person to look into millions of other clients. We want to have some sort of exclusivity where this person can truly devote to us. And we will be providing the correct workload in order to compensate for the fact that "exclusivity" or PRIORITY is given.

title: AI Automation Developer — E-commerce Order Processing (GP · n8n · Playwright · Power Automate)
description: AI Automation Developer — E-commerce Order Processing (GP · n8n · Playwright · Power Automate) Overview Well Traveled Living (WTL) is a Florida-based outdoor products company selling across 20+ retail channels — Amazon Vendor Central, Amazon Seller Central, Wayfair, Home Depot, Lowe's, Costco, Walmart, Menards, and others. Our daily order processing is almost entirely manual: a single team member spends 5–6 hours per day moving orders between our ERP (Microsoft Dynamics GP), a shipping label app (ShipRush), EDI middleware (True Commerce/Foundry), and a mix of retail portals. We need an experienced automation developer to build a 4-phase system that eliminates 60–70% of that daily manual work. This is not a simple Zapier project — it requires real API integration, browser automation, desktop RPA, and an AI agent layer. Phase 1 — API Integrations (start here, ~25 hrs) Amazon SP-API: automated tracking confirmation feeds (POST_ORDER_FULFILLMENT_DATA) for Vendor Central and Seller Central orders Shopify API: end-of-day fulfillment closeouts with tracking numbers Walmart Seller API: tracking submission for closed orders FTP automation: replace Filezilla manual uploads with scripted daily inventory file pushes Dropbox API + Microsoft Graph: auto-compile DCG daily package (Excel + PDFs), upload to Dropbox, generate share link, draft and send email to DCG contact Orchestrate all of the above in n8n with error handling, retry logic, and failure alerts via email or Telegram Phase 2 — Browser Automation (Playwright, ~50 hrs) True Commerce/Foundry: scheduled morning export of EDI orders and Amazon Dropship orders Portal order acknowledgments: Overstock, Logic Broker (BBQ Guys/Full Beauty), Best Buy, Acenda/Target Plus, Sears, Walmart Seller Commerce Hub (CHUB): pack slip retrieval for Home Depot, Costco, Lowe's; UCC label logic for multi-carton Lowe's orders; end-of-day tracking close for Lowe's store orders DSCO: inventory quantity updates for Chewy, Bass Pro, Kohl's 35-portal daily inventory push (mix of file uploads and manual portal logins) Phase 3 — Desktop RPA for GP & ShipRush (Power Automate Desktop, ~70 hrs) Automate the 3-step Order Processing Program (Move to Consignment, Move with Low Qty, Duplicate PO Check) GP SmartList query, export, and rules engine: flag LTL items by specific item numbers, flag incorrect warehouse assignments, identify Wayfair C-batch POs, split Kohl's batches by carrier and warehouse using established naming conventions Sales Batch Entry allocation loop across all daily batches ShipRush Batch label runs: UPS batches (default settings), FedEx batches (uncheck Residential Box), loop per batch with close/reopen requirement Morning exception report: any item requiring human judgment flagged and delivered via email or Telegram before 8:30 AM Bonus if applicable: GP ODBC direct SQL read access for SmartList data instead of GUI automation Phase 4 — AI Agent Layer ( OpenClaw + Claude API, ~35 hrs) Warehouse routing assistant: ZIP-code-based recommendation engine for MAIN (FL) vs CHINO (CA) on dual-warehouse items Email order parser: monitor inbox for manually emailed orders, extract order details, draft GP entry for human review and approval Morning operations dashboard: daily briefing delivered via email or Telegram with flagged exceptions, batch totals, and overnight automation failure alerts Required Skills Python, n8n, Playwright, Amazon SP-API, REST API integration, error handling and retry logic, desktop RPA Preferred Bonus Skills Microsoft Dynamics GP (Great Plains), Power Automate Desktop, True Commerce/Foundry, Commerce Hub (CHUB), ShipRush, OpenClaw , Claude/Anthropic API, DSCO How to Apply — Read This Before Responding Generic proposals will be declined. To be considered, your response must answer all three of the following questions: 1. GP/ERP experience: Have you worked with Microsoft Dynamics GP or another legacy desktop ERP? If yes, describe how. If no, describe how you have handled desktop application automation without an API. 2. Amazon SP-API: Have you built SP-API integrations before? Which feed types or endpoints have you worked with? If not SP-API specifically, describe your Amazon API experience. 3. Phase 1 approach: Walk us through how you would build the Amazon tracking confirmation automation in Phase 1 — what the architecture looks like and how you would handle failures. Proposals that answer all three questions will receive a response within 24 hours. Proposals that do not will not.

Proposals that don't answer these 5 questions will be auto-rejected. ─── What Success Looks Like Week 1-2: • Get full GitLab/K8s access • Fix your first small bug with Pavel's guidance • Learn the AI agent workflow Week 3-4: • Independently debug and fix a production issue • Document what you learned • Start collaborating with our AI agents Month 2: • Own a subsystem (e.g., donor tagging or campaign sends) • Respond to production bugs without escalation • Propose optimizations or improvements Month 3: • Full ownership of your areas • Mentor AI agents with new documentation • Innovate on our AI-augmented workflow ─── Our Screening Process 1. title: Build a 4-Agent AI Automation System — Full Developer Spec Attached, Ready to Build
description: Overview I'm looking for a skilled AI and automation developer to build a system called OpenClaw . It's a 4-agent AI setup that runs a digital agency almost entirely on its own — handling lead generation, sales, website builds, and client management without constant manual input. This is not a vague brief. The full blueprint is already written and attached to this listing as a PDF. It's a complete developer specification, version 4.0, and it covers everything — every agent, every API, every workflow, every database schema, and every integration required. If you know what you're doing, you can pick it up and start building from day one. Before You Apply — Please Read This The attached PDF is the job. Download it. Read it properly. Everything that needs to be built is inside that document. It includes all four agent roles and permission levels, the full CRM schema, every API endpoint, the AWS infrastructure setup, JavaScript and Node.js code templates, workflow diagrams, and all 28 system change notes. If your proposal doesn't reference specific details from the document, I won't be responding to it. I've put a lot of work into that spec and I need someone who respects that and actually reads it before reaching out. What's Being Built The system runs on four AI agents built on the Claude API, each with a specific job. Felix is the orchestrator. He runs the whole operation, routes commands to the other agents, and manages the system's memory and permissions. Remy is the marketer. He handles lead generation, social media content, and email outreach campaigns. Iris is the account manager. She sends proposals, closes deals, processes payments, and onboards new clients. Codex is the builder. He builds website prototypes, handles urgent fixes, and manages the development workflow. Together they handle the full client journey from initial lead capture through to a live website and an ongoing monthly retainer, with as little manual involvement from me as possible. The revenue target is $10,000 or more per month. Flexibility is Required — Please Read This Too The spec document is the foundation, but this is a living system. As we build, things will come up. Requirements may be added, workflows may need adjusting, and the scope will likely shift as the system gets tested in the real world. I need someone who is comfortable with that. I am not looking for a developer who builds exactly what's on the page and then disappears. I need someone who can think on their feet, communicate clearly when something needs to change, and adapt without making it a big deal. If you need everything locked down in writing before you'll touch anything, this probably isn't the right project for you. If you enjoy building something real from the ground up, iterating as it develops, and being part of something that's genuinely growing — this is a good project to be involved in. Working Setup The system will be built on my 2019 iMac. I'll give you full remote access so you can work directly on my machine, set everything up properly, and I can keep eyes on what's being built at all times. I'm open to whichever remote access tool you prefer. TeamViewer, AnyDesk, Chrome Remote Desktop, and macOS Screen Sharing are all options. Just mention what you're comfortable with in your proposal and we'll get it sorted before we start. Required Skills AI agent development using Claude API or similar large language models Node.js or Python for API integrations and automation logic AWS, specifically ECS Fargate, S3, EBS, and CloudWatch Google Sheets API Stripe and PandaDoc API REST API integrations across multiple services Zapier or equivalent automation tooling GitHub for version control and automated backups Proven experience building multi-agent or orchestration systems Comfortable doing development work via remote desktop To Apply Answer both of these questions in your proposal. If you skip them, I won't reply. First, based on the attached spec, how would you approach this build? Break it down into phases and give me a realistic timeline. Second, what remote access tool do you prefer when working on a Mac, and have you done remote development on a client's machine before? Project Type: Fixed price or milestone-based, open to your recommendation Environment: 2019 iMac with full remote access provided Spec Document: Attached — OpenClaw 4-Agent System Developer Specification v4.0. This document is the full scope of work. Read it before applying.

title: Industrial Talent Sourcing & Agency Automation System
description: 1. Project Overview The objective of this project is to build a proprietary, automated talent-sourcing and business development engine ("The System"). The system will operate from a local Mac Mini (M-Series) environment and use a combination of information look-up, AI-driven technical scoring, and CRM automation to identify, vet, and place high-end CNC and Robotics technicians within the Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) metroplex. 2. Technical Stack Requirements The professional must be proficient in the following: • Primary Language: Python (Playwright/Puppeteer, BeautifulSoup). • OpenClaw • Database: PostgreSQL (Self-hosted via Docker). • AI/LLM: Ollama (Local Llama 3) for technical post-analysis. • CRM/ATS Integration: Login and system TBD. • Enrichment/Outreach: User and Business Access System Login. 3. Scope of Work Phase A: The "Talent Claw" (Lookup) • Forum Access and Review: Develop robust information lookup for specialized message boards (e.g., Robot-Forum, Practical Machinist). • Keyword Filtering: Implement logic to prioritize users based on DFW-specific location markers (e.g., Dallas, Fort Worth area codes). • Data Extraction: Capture usernames, post history, and stated machine specialties (e.g., Fanuc, Haas, 5-Axis). Phase B: Technical Scoring Engine • Local AI Integration: Connect the information access bot to a local Ollama instance. • Vetting Logic: Create a prompt-based scoring system that analyzes a user's recent technical contributions to assign an "Expertise Score" (1-10). • Profile Enrichment: Integrate with user identifcation system to cross-reference forum handles with LinkedIn profiles and verified contact information. Phase C: The Client & CRM Pipeline • Employer Sourcing: Log into tools for DFW machine shop and add to database. • Database Synchronization: Ensure all talent and client leads are automatically loaded into a local PostgreSQL database and mirrored to the Recruit CRM. • Automated Outreach: Set up "First-Touch" email sequences in Instantly.ai that trigger when a high-score technician is matched to a local job opening. 4. Deliverables 1. Localized Python Scripts: Fully commented code for data gathering, scoring, and syncing. 2. Docker Configuration: A docker-compose.yml file to launch the Database and GUI (NocoDB) on the Mac Mini. 3. System Access Maps: Documentation for how the local bot communicates with User, Business Inventory System, and the CRM. 4. Operational Dashboard: A basic visual interface (via NocoDB or Streamlit) to monitor bot activity and lead counts. 5. Project Timeline & Milestones • Milestone 1: Successful local Database setup and initial Forum Data Gather (7-10 Days). • Milestone 2: AI Scoring logic and Profile Enrichment integration (10-14 Days). • Milestone 3: Full CRM sync and Outreach automation testing (7 Days).

(Telegram preferred but not required) Player classification system Players should be automatically classified into levels such as: • Fish / Pure Fish • Recreational Players • Weak Regular • Regular • Winning Regular • Professional • Suspicious Players Statistical indicators used for analysis should include but are not limited to: Preflop metrics • VPIP • PFR • 3Bet • Fold to 3Bet • 4Bet • Fold to 4Bet • Limp • Limp Fold • Limp Call • Limp Raise • Position-based VPIP and PFR Postflop metrics • Cbet Flop • Cbet Turn • Fold to Cbet • Aggression Factor • Aggression % • WWSF • WTSD • W$SD Winning indicators • BB/100 • Net winnings • Sample size (number of hands) • Win rate trends over time Behavioral analysis and anomaly detection The system should detect suspicious patterns including: • Possible AI usage • Ghosting (multiple players controlling one account) • Collusion behavior • Extremely consistent decision patterns • Unnatural statistical stability Data manipulation detection The system should also detect players intentionally manipulating their statistics, such as: • Playing extremely loose at lower stakes to increase VPIP • Playing normally or tightly at higher stakes • Artificially changing play style across different stakes Session and activity monitoring The system should also analyze: • Session length • Playing time patterns • Stake distribution • Decision speed consistency • Activity patterns across different tables Server and data architecture • Player data should be stored on a server database • The system should support real-time analysis • The system should allow querying player data by player ID • API or query interface preferred Optional integrations Integration with tools such as: • OpenClaw • ChatGPT • Telegram bot These tools can be used to query player data or generate reports. Preferred background Developers with experience in: • Poker analytics • Data science • Behavioral analysis • Risk control systems • Gaming platforms are strongly preferred. Timeline We would like this system to be completed as soon as possible and are willing to increase the budget for the right developer.

title: Lead AI Agent Developer — Manage Claude Code Dev Team for E-commerce Brand
description: We're a fast-growing D2C women's wellness supplement brand running ~8,000 active subscribers. We're building an AI Agent intelligence system that scrapes, analyzes, and reports on our market, competitors, customers, and ad performance — automatically, every week. We have 3 developers building pipelines with Claude Code, OpenClaw , Apify, and Supabase. We need a senior dev who builds alongside them but also owns the overall quality of the system. Someone who actively monitors what the other devs are producing, catches problems before they reach us, and makes sure this bot becomes something we can genuinely trust. Your role: You're the team lead dev, you mainly make sure the other devs build theirs properly. You make your own workflows for the bot, but you also make sure other devs are building quality. You're actively reviewing their work, checking their output, testing their pipelines once they say it's done, and flagging issues. If a report has bad data, you catch it. If a schema won't scale, you fix it. If a scraper is going to break next week, you see it coming. Your goal is simple: make this bot as good as it can possibly be. Every report it produces should be something we can act on without second-guessing. Every pipeline should run reliably without someone babysitting it. You own that standard. You're responsible to make this AI agent the best it can be. WHAT YOU'LL DO - Build your own pipelines and workflows alongside the team - Review all pipeline output: does the data check out, are conclusions backed by evidence, is the report useful - Manage 3 developers: who works on what, monitor the whole system - Improve architecture across the whole system: schema design, prompt engineering, error handling, monitoring What you need: - Deep experience building AI automation systems with real data pipelines, not just chatbots - Hands-on Claude Code experience or equivalent LLM-powered development - Strong Supabase/Postgres knowledge - Understanding of e-commerce and performance marketing: ROAS, creative testing, ad account structure, retention metrics. Or ATLEAST know how to build reports around them. - You care about data quality to the point where a hallucinated number in a report genuinely bothers you Nice to have: - Experience with Meta Ads API - Background in D2C or supplement/wellness brands - Familiarity with Klaviyo, Shopify, or Triple Whale STRUCTURE Starting part-time at 15-20 hours/week, growing to full-time as the system scales. Long-term role. Rate depends on experience. IN YOUR PROPOSAL - You inherit a pipeline that scrapes data weekly and produces a report. The founder says the insights feel shallow and repetitive. You discover it's only pulling 200 data points per run. Walk us through what you'd do: how much data should it pull, how do you ensure Claude can digest high volumes without losing quality, and what would you change to make the output actually trustworthy. - How do you prevent a Claude-generated report from containing hallucinated numbers or false confidence? What specific checks do you build into the system so the founder never has to wonder if a data point is real. IMPORTANT: We will immediately skip any proposal that reads like it was written by AI. If you're serious about this, we have a very nice and long term position to make this AI agent great with us together.

title: Chief Executive Officer
description: Fractional CEO (Growth) | Three-Track Services Group | AI-First | Remote Vision Group | Offshore | Fractional to start | |20-30 hrs/week Read This Before Applying This is not a corporate CEO role. There are no decks to present and no politics to navigate. This is an execution seat at the growth layer of a multi-entity services group that is building something genuinely different. If you have done consulting, startups, and acquisitions, and you understand how each one works at the unit level, keep reading. If you have only done one of the three, this is not for you yet. About Vision Group Vision Group is an AI-enabled holding company targeting trade and professional service businesses across Australia. Our avatar businesses include plumbers, electricians, roofers, HVAC operators, remedial builders, concreters, pest controllers, landscapers, architects, engineers, surveyors, certifiers, and related professionals. We build, consult into, and acquire these businesses. Then we supercharge them using OpenClaw (our AI orchestration layer), a 63-source go-to-market system, GHL automation, and lean offshore teams. The internal marketing engine is Digital Grace Marketing (DGM). The operating system is Monday.com. One of our portfolio startups, Primeline, is tracking $1.1M to $1.4M revenue from a standing start. That is what the model produces when it works. We are now building the growth leadership layer to run three tracks simultaneously. The Three Growth Tracks Track 1: Consulting We go into avatar businesses, assess their operations and maturity, and match them to the right level of our product offering across marketing, admin, operations, and financials. We implement using our systems and team. We take fees. The Head of Consulting owns this track. Track 2: Startups We identify underserved niches in trade and professional services, find the right operator, and stand up net-new businesses using our infrastructure. The Head of Startups owns this track. Track 3: Acquisitions We source, assess, and acquire businesses in the $600K to $1M range. We also take equity stakes in businesses we have built relationships with, including consulting clients who are ready for that conversation. The Head of Acquisitions owns this track. The three tracks support each other and are measured individually on ROIC. They share infrastructure, teams, and systems. They bootstrap each other. As capital accumulates, we deploy it back into people, technology, and further equity. The CEO Role You sit between the founders and the three track heads. You are not building the infrastructure (that is Neal's domain). You are not setting the strategy (that is NC's domain). You are the chess player who makes the three tracks move, determines the minimum viable product for each, identifies the tipping points, and makes sure nothing important falls through the gaps. What you will actually do • Manage and support the Head of Consulting, Head of Startups, and Head of Acquisitions. • Determine the MVP for each track and define what is needed at the lowest viable level to deliver the service, find the operator, or source the deal. • Identify when a consulting relationship is ready to be handed to Acquisitions for an equity conversation. You decide the timing, not the track heads. • Set KPIs for each track, build dashboards, and report against them weekly to the founders. • Gather data from all three tracks and use it to inform which track deserves capital and attention at any given point. • Work inside OpenClaw , Monday.com, and GHL. You do not need to be the builder of these systems. You need to direct people who run them and use outputs to make decisions. • Identify blockages early, delegate resolution to the relevant track head, and supervise through to completion. Blockages do not reach the founders. • Direct each track head to analyse the avatar business models they are working with, so the messaging, product level, and offer match the maturity and needs of each business. You quality-check this, you do not do it for them. What We Are Looking For • You have direct experience across all three: consulting into businesses, building a startup from scratch, and doing deals or acquisitions. • You understand unit economics at each stage of maturity and you know what a minimum viable product looks like for each track. • You are comfortable analysing a small business quickly, identifying its model, and matching it to the right offer. • You are AI-willing. You do not need to be a technical AI expert. You need to be comfortable working with AI tools, directing people who run them, and using outputs to make faster, better decisions. • You can manage three people who are at completely different stages of business development simultaneously without losing the thread on any of them. • You communicate clearly and do not need hand-holding. You bring solutions, not problems. • You are offshore-friendly. Most of the team is offshore. What We Are Not Looking For • Strategy consultants who produce frameworks and disappear. • People who have only done one of the three tracks and are theorising about the others. • Anyone who needs a fully built machine before they can start. Part of this role is defining what the machine needs to look like. • Anyone who treats AI as a threat or a gimmick. Engagement Rate: USD $20 to $30 per hour Hours: 20 hours per week to start Structure: Fractional. Path to full-time as tracks mature and revenue grows Location: Remote Reports to: founders How to Apply Send a short cover note, not a template, that answers all three of the following: • Which of the three tracks (consulting, startup, acquisitions) have you done and what was the measurable result in each? • Describe a time you managed people at different stages of business maturity at the same time. What broke first and how did you fix it? • What AI tools do you currently use in your work and how do they change the way you operate? Applications that do not answer all three will not be reviewed.

title: AI Agent Automation Specialist Needed
description: I'm looking for a hands-on AI engineer or strong full-stack builder to design and implement a flexible multi-agent AI system that can automate recurring research, analysis, and decision-support workflows. This project is a good fit for candidates who want real-world experience building practical AI agent systems. Strong junior candidates are welcome if they can build fast, think clearly, and work independently. This can be a strong paid internship-style opportunity for someone who wants to sharpen applied AI engineering skills. The goal is to build a configurable platform where AI agents can work together in sequences or flows. Each agent should be easy to define, modify, add, remove, or connect to other agents without heavy engineering work. Example Use Cases AI news analyst workflow - One agent collects and reads weekly agentic AI news, blog posts, papers, and social posts - Another agent summarizes the key updates with source links - Another agent reviews all summaries and produces a more opinionated synthesis, lessons learned, and key trends Stock research workflow - One agent searches daily for ticker symbols and promising short-term or mid-term stock opportunities - Another agent reviews, filters, and analyzes the candidates - Another agent can shortlist strong bets, reject weak ideas, or escalate uncertain cases for review These are examples only. The system must be general and reusable for many workflows. What We Need Built The system should support: - Configurable agent workflows - Configurable frequency and scheduling - Configurable tasks, roles, prompts, and tool usage - Easy prompt editing - Easy addition of new agents - Easy chaining of agent outputs into downstream agents - Clear separation between configuration and code Support for multiple LLM providers: - OpenAI - Claude - Gemini etc Technical Preferences Framework is optional, but preference is for the simplest and most maintainable solution. OpenClaw with security protocols accepted. Langgraph is fine. The ideal system is one that even a technical product manager can understand, edit, and extend. Preference for: - Clean architecture - Clear config-driven design - Easy local setup - Good documentation - Practical over over-engineered You may use AI coding tools during development. However, all prompts used for AI-assisted coding must be saved and submitted with the final project as part of project documentation. Repository / Work Requirements - GitHub access will be provided - All work must be submitted in GitHub - Commits should be clean and reasonably organized - Final handoff must include working code, setup steps, and documentation Expected Deliverables At minimum, the contractor should deliver: - A working multi-agent system with configurable workflows - Support for OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini - A configuration layer for: workflow steps, agent definitions, prompts, schedule/frequency and agent interactions At least 1 implemented example workflows: agentic AI learning and news workflow. Second workflow is optional but preferred. Documentation covering: - architecture - setup - how to add a new agent - how to change prompts - how to create a new workflow - A saved record of all AI coding prompts used during the build, README and handoff notes so another engineer or PM can continue the work Who This Is Good For - AI engineers and interns - Full-stack engineers interested in AI systems - applied ML engineers - ambitious junior contractors who want real project experience - candidates who want a paid project that also builds their AI portfolio Your BID can be lower than what provided if you are unexperienced and you want to learn in a job.

title: BH SEO Intelligence
description: Hi Muhammad Anan, I’m looking for a senior AI engineer to help finalize and launch the MVP of an AI-driven SaaS platform I have already been developing. I operate behavioral health treatment programs in the U.S., and this platform is designed specifically for marketing intelligence in the behavioral health industry. **Project name:** BH SEO Intelligence Much of the platform has already been prototyped and the MVP site is live, but I need help refining the workflow, stabilizing the system, and preparing it for subscribers. The FREE product is live. The upsell is live; however, the remaining subscription products and the comprehensive report product need workflow, tasking and development. **URL:**https://www.bhseointelligence.com **Objective** Expand an AI-powered SEO intelligence and workflow platform that audits behavioral health provider websites and generates marketing insights, keyword strategies, and actionable task workflows. The system will support both: • one-time SEO audit purchases • subscription monitoring and task generation **Current status** The core concept and much of the functionality have already been built and tested. At this point I need an experienced engineer to help: • review the existing system • identify gaps and shore up the system for this MVP phase, before marketing & prospecting funnels go live • finalize next-phase workflows • test reliability • prepare the system for full deployment **MVP functionality** 1. **SEO Audit Engine** * Crawl a customer’s website * Evaluate technical SEO, content structure, and keyword coverage * Identify SEO issues and optimization opportunities 2. **Competitor Analysis** * Identify top competitor websites * Compare keyword coverage and content gaps * Generate competitor intelligence insights 3. **Keyword Opportunity Analysis** * Identify high-value behavioral health search terms * Score keywords by opportunity and difficulty * Map keywords to recommended service pages or content 4. **Marketing & Content Analysis** * Evaluate on-page SEO and service page structure * Identify missing pages or content gaps 5. **AI Task Generation** * Convert analysis into prioritized marketing tasks suggested to customers * Example outputs: * “Create service page targeting ‘detox near Atlanta’” * “Improve internal linking for PHP program pages” 6. **Report Generation** * Generate professional reports for one-time audit customers * Subscription users receive ongoing SEO monitoring and task workflows 7. **User Dashboard** * Simple dashboard where customers can: * run audits * view reports * see prioritized tasks & mark each as completed **Technical goals** Phase 1 – MVP Completion • Review current system architecture • finalize the workflow for SEO analysis and reporting, lead gen, & marketing • identify remaining tasks required for MVP launch • help troubleshoot existing code • improve reliability and performance Phase 2 – Testing Environment Test workflows across different environments: • Perplexity Computer • OpenClaw (local testing) The goal is to identify the most efficient development and testing workflow. Phase 3 – Production Deployment Prepare the platform for re-deployment on a cloud/VPS environment with: • containerized re-deployment (Docker) • production database • secure API management • scalable architecture **Preferred stack (flexible)** • Python or Node backend • Docker containers • PostgreSQL database • AI model APIs (OpenAI / Claude etc.) • automation workflows (possibly n8n) **What I’m looking for** An engineer who can: 1. Review the existing MVP system and architecture 2. Help define the minimal feature set required for an MVP marketing launch 3. Assist with debugging and workflow improvements 4. Prepare the platform for deployment to production infrastructure **First step** I’d like to begin with a **paid architecture and system review phase**, where you: • evaluate the existing platform • identify the remaining requirements for an MVP marketing-ready launch • propose a clean architecture for production deployment **Questions for you** • How would you architect a system like this? • What tools or libraries would you use for the SEO crawling and analysis layer? • How would you structure the system so it does not depend on Perplexity Computer and can run independently on a VPS/cloud server? • Have you built AI-driven SaaS platforms or automation systems before? If it looks like a good fit, we can move from the architecture phase into completing the MVP and deployment. Looking forward to hearing your thoughts.

title: AI Platform Architect — Industrial Multi-Agent System (Python, LLM, API)

Please review attached documents for further explination. We will break this project down into phases. Phase 1 - Freight, Warehouse, Accounting. - Please see attached files for surther details. We need a hands-on developer responsible for building, maintaining, and scaling our multi-agent AI platform. You'll work directly with the CEO and leadership team to turn operational pain points into automated solutions. This is a ground-floor opportunity to architect an AI-native ERP system for manufacturing. Agent Development & Orchestration • Build, deploy, SECURE, and maintain specialized AI agents (e.g., financial reporting, production monitoring, logistics optimization, quality management) • Design inter-agent communication protocols — how bots delegate, escalate, and share data • Implement deterministic automation pipelines (Python scripts, scheduled jobs) alongside LLM-powered decision-making • Manage agent memory, context persistence, and instruction fidelity across sessions Data Engineering & Integration • Build and maintain integrations with Microsoft Graph API (email, calendars, OneDrive/SharePoint), manufacturing systems (Limble CMMS, BatchMaster/SAP B1), and logistics platforms (FreightView) • Design data pipelines from production floor → agents → executive dashboards • Parse, validate, and transform operational data (payroll exports, production logs, inventory reports) • Build and maintain master data files (employee rosters, equipment databases, production targets) Infrastructure & Security • Build a fleet of Mac Mini compute nodes connected via Tailscale VPN. Localize and reduce token usage. • Implement authentication, secrets management, and access control across the agent network • Design failure recovery, health monitoring, and alerting for agent uptime • Ensure data privacy and security — employee data, financial data, production data • Build audit trails and logging for all automated actions Manufacturing Operations Support • Automate daily reporting: OT analysis, production KPIs, equipment downtime, quality metrics • Build tools for production scheduling, inventory management, and capacity planning • Develop anomaly detection and alerting for production line issues • Create board-ready reporting automation for PE stakeholders What We're Looking For Must-Have • 3+ years Python development (scripting, automation, data processing) • Experience with REST APIs and data integration (Microsoft Graph API, OAuth2, webhooks) • Familiarity with LLMs/AI agents — prompt engineering, context management, tool use, function calling • Strong understanding of networking fundamentals (VPN, SSH, firewall configuration) • Experience with JSON/XLSX data processing and transformation • Comfort with macOS/Linux command line and remote server management • Self-starter who can own projects end-to-end with minimal supervision Strong Plus • Experience with multi-agent AI systems or orchestration frameworks • Manufacturing, ERP, or operations technology background • Microsoft 365 / SharePoint / Power Platform experience • Experience with CMMS systems (Limble, Fiix, UpKeep) • Knowledge of cGMP, FDA regulations, or supplement/beverage manufacturing • Database design (SQL or document stores) • Experience with Tailscale, WireGuard, or zero-trust networking • CI/CD, version control, deployment automation Mindset • Resourceful over theoretical — we need someone who ships, not someone who architectures in a vacuum • Security-conscious — you'll have access to financial data, employee records, and production systems • Bias toward automation — if a human is doing it repeatedly, you should be asking why a bot isn't • Clear communicator — you'll interface directly with the CEO, VP of Ops, and HR. No jargon walls. • Manufacturing curiosity — you don't need to be an expert, but you need to care about how things get made Tech Stack (Current) • AI Platform: OpenClaw (multi-agent orchestration) • LLMs: Anthropic Claude (Opus, Sonnet) • Language: Python 3.x (primary), Bash • APIs: Microsoft Graph, Limble CMMS, FreightView, custom REST • Infrastructure: Mac Minis (Apple Silicon), Tailscale mesh VPN • Messaging: Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, GroupMe (multi-channel bot presence) • Data: JSON, XLSX, OneDrive/SharePoint, email parsing • Monitoring: Custom agent health checks, cron-based scheduling

title: AI Engineer for DTC Retention Marketing Agency
description: Who we are We are a retention marketing agency. We build email, SMS, subscription, and lifecycle programmes for DTC e-commerce brands using Klaviyo and Shopify. Our team is fully remote, async-first, and runs lean. We currently operate in pods: strategist, coordinator, designer, copywriter. Our goal over the next 12 months is to compress those pods down to one or two people max, using AI and automation to replace the manual steps, not the thinking. That's where you come in. --- What this role actually is We're not looking for someone to "set up custom GPTs" for us. We need an engineer who can look at how a retention marketing agency actually operates - the briefs, the builds, the reporting, the QA loops - and systematically identify where automation creates real leverage. You'll embed yourself in our workflows, understand how work moves through our team, and build the automations that let fewer people deliver more, faster, and at higher quality. This is a hands-on building role, not a consulting or advisory one. You'll be expected to ship working solutions, not slide decks. --- The work Here's where we see the biggest opportunities. We expect you to validate, challenge, and add to this list: 1. Creative production (biggest priority) Our current process: strategist writes a brief, copywriter drafts copy, designer builds the email/popup/SMS creative, strategist reviews and submits to client for review & revisions. That's five handoffs before anything gets into Klaviyo. We want to explore how AI can take an approved brief and generate an initial design draft - copy, layout, imagery - that a human then refines. Not perfect output. A strong first pass that cuts production time dramatically. 2. Data analysis and reporting Our coordinators currently pull data manually from Klaviyo/Hiro Analytics, build pulsetake spreadsheets, and assemble performance decks, for the strategist to then analyse and build recommendations from. This is time-intensive, repetitive, and follows predictable patterns. We want to automate as much of the data extraction, formatting, and insight generation as possible so strategists spend their time on interpretation and client communication, not data entry. 3. Workflow automation Brief generation, QA checklists, task creation, campaign scheduling checks - there are dozens of small, repeatable processes that could be templated or automated. We need someone who spots these and builds solutions without being asked. 4. Client-facing AI tools Custom projects, internal agents, or lightweight tools that help our team (and potentially our clients) move faster. We already use some of these. We want someone who can take them further. A 24/7 AI strategist agent that clients can consult with at any point would be great. 5. Client context gathering Exploring the idea of using the likes of Openclaw to continuously scrape Slack, client calls, and other data sources to assess client sentiment and give strategists an wlayd up to date list of tasks/initiatives/considerations for each client. --- What we need from you Required: - Proven experience building AI/automation solutions in a professional context - agency, e-commerce, or SaaS. Not hobby projects. We need someone who's automated real business processes for real teams. - Strong technical skills: Python, APIs, prompt engineering, and familiarity with tools like Make/Zapier, OpenAI API, Claude API, Langchain, or similar. You should be comfortable building custom integrations, not just connecting pre-built modules. - Understanding of email marketing or e-commerce operations. You don't need to be a Klaviyo expert, but you need to understand what a lifecycle flow is, why a campaign brief exists, and how creative production works in practice. - Ability to work autonomously. We'll give you context and access. We expect you to come back with "here's what I found, here's what I'd build, here's how long it'll take" - not wait for a task list. - Clear communication in English. Async-first means your written updates, documentation, and proposals need to be sharp. Strong signals (not required but they'll move you to the top): - You've worked inside or alongside a digital marketing agency - You've built automation for creative production workflows (design, copy, or both) - You have experience with Klaviyo's API - You've built internal tools or dashboards using AI - You can show before/after metrics on time or cost savings from something you've built --- What this is NOT Let's save us both time: - This is not a "prompt engineering" role where you write prompts and call it a day. - This is not a one-off project. We want someone who grows with us as our automation needs evolve. - This is not a role for someone who's been "doing AI" for three months and lists every buzzword on their profile. We'll ask you to walk us through what you've built and why. - This is not agency strategy work. We have strategists. You're the engineer who makes them faster. --- How to apply Skip the generic cover letter. Instead, tell us: 1. One specific automation or AI solution you've built for a business (not a personal project). What was the problem, what did you build, and what was the measurable result? 2. Look at our setup - a retention marketing agency with strategists, coordinators, copywriters, and designers working in Klaviyo for DTC brands. Where would you start? What's the first thing you'd want to automate and why? 3. Your hourly rate and weekly availability. If you can't answer questions 1 and 2 with specifics, this probably isn't the right fit. If you can, we'd genuinely like to hear from you.

title: Website Redesign & AI Search Optimization
description: AI Search Optimization & Website Redesign — Medical Imaging Equipment Company (30+ Years in Business) We are looking for an experienced web developer and AI search optimization specialist to transform our existing website into a platform that is fully visible and highly ranked across all AI-powered search engines, traditional search engines, and emerging AI discovery platforms. This is not a standard SEO job. We need someone who understands how AI models like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, and Microsoft Copilot source, evaluate, and surface business information — and who can redesign and architect our website to ensure Amber Diagnostics appears in those AI-generated answers when someone asks about used medical imaging equipment, mobile MRI rentals, refurbished CT scanners, or any related query. What We Need Our current website needs to be redesigned with modern, creative, professional design while simultaneously being rebuilt for AI discoverability. This includes but is not limited to: Implementing structured data and schema markup (JSON-LD) across the entire site — organization schema, product schema, FAQ schema, service schema, local business schema, and any other relevant structured data types that AI crawlers and large language models use to understand and recommend businesses. AI-optimized content strategy including keyword research specifically targeting how AI platforms interpret and serve results — not just traditional Google keyword rankings but the natural language queries and conversational phrases that users are asking AI chatbots. Creating and maintaining a blog strategy with authoritative, well-written articles that position Amber Diagnostics as an industry thought leader in pre-owned medical imaging equipment — covering topics like MRI pricing, mobile imaging rental guides, equipment buying decisions, site planning, and total cost of ownership. Optimizing meta descriptions, title tags, header hierarchy, alt text, and internal linking specifically for AI crawlability and knowledge graph inclusion. Ensuring our site content is structured in a way that AI models can extract clean, citation-worthy answers — meaning clear, factual, well-organized content that an AI would confidently reference when generating a response for a user. Implementing and maintaining an XML sitemap, robots.txt optimization, and crawl directives that favor both traditional search engine bots and AI training crawlers (such as GPTBot, ClaudeBot, and PerplexityBot). Page speed optimization, mobile responsiveness, Core Web Vitals compliance, and overall technical health to meet current ranking and indexing standards. Setting up proper canonical tags, Open Graph tags, and Twitter Card markup so our content is properly represented when shared or referenced across any platform. Integrating review and reputation markup where applicable to strengthen trust signals that AI models weigh when recommending businesses. Ongoing Content and Visibility Beyond the initial build, we need someone who can provide ongoing support including loading regular blog content, monitoring our visibility across AI search platforms, tracking which AI chatbots are surfacing our content and which are not, adjusting strategy as AI search algorithms evolve, and producing monthly reports showing our AI search presence and progress. Technical Environment and Collaboration You should be comfortable working in or alongside the following: Our website platform and CMS (current site will need evaluation and potential migration recommendation if warranted). Salesforce CRM — our customer and inventory data lives in Salesforce and there may be opportunities to pull dynamic content, inventory listings, or lead capture forms that connect directly to our CRM. Experience with Salesforce web-to-lead, API integrations, or embedded Salesforce data is a strong plus. Pardot (Marketing Cloud Account Engagement) — our email drip campaigns run through Pardot, and any lead capture, form submissions, or landing pages on the website need to integrate cleanly with our existing Pardot campaigns without creating conflicts or duplicate records. Moltbot (formerly Clawdbot/ OpenClaw ) — we are implementing a Moltbot AI automation platform that will be scraping data and matching buyers and sellers across multiple sources including our website. Familiarity with Moltbot or willingness to coordinate with our Moltbot developer to ensure the website's data structure, content, and markup are optimized for AI agent consumption is important. SendGrid — we use SendGrid for certain email workflows, so understanding how website-triggered emails interact with both SendGrid and Pardot is valuable. Google Analytics 4, Google Search Console, and Google Business Profile — full setup, configuration, and ongoing monitoring. Who We Are Looking For An experienced web developer and designer who brings creative, modern design sensibility — we don't want a generic template site, we want something that reflects 30+ years of industry expertise and looks like it belongs to a company that knows what it's doing. Someone with proven, demonstrable experience in AI search optimization — not just traditional SEO. We want to see examples or case studies of how you've helped businesses appear in AI-generated search results. A strong understanding of how large language models consume and rank web content, including the role of structured data, E-E-A-T signals (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness), and content architecture. Experience in B2B or industrial/medical equipment markets is a significant plus but not required if you can demonstrate the technical skills and willingness to learn our industry. Must be based in the United States. This is a firm requirement. To Apply Please include in your proposal: 1) Examples of websites you've built or redesigned with a focus on AI search visibility. 2) Your approach to making a business visible across AI search platforms — not just Google. 3) Any experience you have working with Salesforce integrations, Pardot, or AI automation platforms. 4) Your availability and estimated timeline for an initial site audit and Phase 1 implementation plan. This is intended to be a long-term partnership, not a one-time project. AI search is evolving rapidly and we need someone who will grow with us and keep us ahead of the curve. If this is all in your wheelhouse... We want to hear from you!

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Java/Spring Boot + MongoDB
description: Senior Backend Engineer - Java/Spring Boot + MongoDB READ CAREFULLY BEFORE APPLYING - We use AI to screen applications. Generic proposals will be auto-rejected. Individual contractors only — no agencies, no subcontracting Quick Facts • Rate: $40-60/hour (negotiable for exceptional candidates) • Hours: 20+/week (long-term, 6+ months) • Start: Within 2 weeks • Remote: Fully remote, any timezone • Required: Java/Spring Boot + MongoDB expert ─── What This Role Is NOT: • Entry-level or mid-level position • Greenfield project (production code with 3 years of history) • Fully documented handoff (previous dev is leaving, minimal overlap) • For developers who can't independently ramp on legacy code • An agency engagement — you will be working directly with our team, not delegating to others What This Role IS: • Senior backend position on a live political fundraising platform • Owning critical parts of the system: campaigns, donor lists, integrations, MongoDB optimization • Production debugging under pressure on complex business logic • Working alongside AI agents and an AI-forward team where you own the quality of what ships ─── Tech Stack Must Have (3+ Years Each): • Java + Spring Boot (complex business logic, production scale — this is the core requirement) • MongoDB (schema design, aggregations, indexing, performance tuning) • REST API integrations (SFTP, webhooks, third-party platforms) • Git workflow (we use GitLab) Nice to Have: • Kubernetes + Docker (our deployments are automated, but ability to improve those automations is a plus) • Node.js / JavaScript • MySQL • RabbitMQ / AWS SQS / Kafka • Grafana / Prometheus stack • Elasticsearch • Hazelcast • DevOps experience • AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor, Copilot — useful for ramping and productivity, not a substitute for engineering judgment) • OpenClaw or similar AI agent frameworks ─── Key Responsibilities 1. Backend Ownership (Java/Spring Boot) • Campaigns and lists module (complex business logic — not simple CRUD) • Campaign state management, send throttling, compliance logic • Production bug fixes and ongoing feature development • Code refactoring and resolving tech debt 2. MongoDB Database Ownership • Supporters database (30M+ records) • List segmentation and queries • Tag assignment logic • Performance optimization and indexing strategies 3. Production Integrations • WinRed donation pipeline (SFTP, revenue-critical) • Tableau based donation pipeline (SFTP, revenue-critical) • Telnyx SMS API • Error handling and monitoring 4. AI-Assisted Development • We're an AI-forward team — AI tools are encouraged for code exploration, documentation, and accelerating your ramp-up • That said: AI writes drafts, you own the quality. Every line that ships gets reviewed by you • You'll collaborate with AI agents (CommandeR, Boris, Andy) as part of the team workflow • Document as you learn — your notes improve the whole team ─── Required Qualifications • 5+ years backend development • 3+ years Java/Spring Boot in production (non-negotiable) • 3+ years MongoDB • API integration experience (SFTP, REST, webhooks) • Self-sufficient: can ramp on legacy code without hand-holding • Availability to respond immediately, off-hours, and weekends to urgent bug fixes. We deploy messages 360 days+ per year. • Production mindset: comfortable with "fix it now" pressure • Clear async communication and documentation habits ─── Screening Questions (MUST ANSWER IN PROPOSAL): 1. Java/Spring Boot: Describe a Java/Spring Boot project you've worked on — what it did, what your role was, and what was the hardest part of the backend logic. 2. MongoDB: Rate your MongoDB skill 1-10. Describe your most complex optimization. 3. Legacy Code: Describe joining a project after the previous dev left. How did you ramp up and how long before you were fully productive? 4. Production Pressure: Tell us about a critical production bug you fixed under time pressure. Walk us through exactly what you did. 5. Availability: When can you start? What hours per week can you commit? Proposals that don't answer all 5 questions will be auto-rejected. ─── What Success Looks Like Week 1-2: GitLab access, first small bug fix with guidance, learn the codebase structure Week 3-4: Independently debug a production issue, document learnings, start collaborating with the team Month 2: Own a subsystem (donor tagging or campaign sends), respond to bugs without escalation, propose optimizations Month 3: Full ownership of your areas, proactively improving systems and documentation ─── Why This Role is Unique • Real Impact: Your code affects millions in political fundraising for major campaigns • AI-Forward Team: We use AI tools to move faster — and we're building the tooling others will use in 2027 • Autonomy: We hire senior people because we trust them. No micromanagement • Teammates, not just tools: Our AI agents (CommandeR, Boris, Andy) are part of the workflow — you'll collaborate with them, not just around them ─── Red Flags (Don't Apply If...): • Your Java/Spring Boot experience is only theoretical or academic • You need extensive documentation to be productive • You can't handle production pressure • You expect AI to write production code without a thorough review • You work through an agency or plan to subcontract this work ─── Compensation & Logistics • Rate: $40-60/hour • Hours: 20+/week, potential for more during high season • Contract: Hourly, Upwork tracking preferred — individual contractors only • Duration: Long-term (6+ months minimum, likely 12+) • Timezone: Flexible, some US Eastern overlap needed for meetings ─── How to Apply Your proposal MUST include: 1. Headline: "Java/Spring Boot + MongoDB - [Your Years of Experience]" 2. Answers to all 5 screening questions 3. GitHub/GitLab portfolio showing Java/Spring Boot work 4. Your hourly rate within the $40-60 range 5. Specific start date and weekly hours available Include "I read the full posting" so we know you're not using a proposal bot. Individual contractors only — if you work through an agency or plan to delegate this work, please do not apply. ─── A Note on Our Clients VoterTrove works exclusively with conservative political campaigns and organizations. Our platform is used to raise funds for Republican candidates and causes. We do not care what your politics are, but if working in conservative political tech is something you're not comfortable with, this isn't the right role — and that's completely okay. We'd rather you know upfront than find out on day one. If it's not an issue, we'd love to hear from you. About VoterTrove: We're a political fundraising platform helping campaigns raise millions through email and SMS. Our stack is modern, our team is senior, and we move fast. If you're a strong Java/Spring Boot engineer who takes ownership seriously, we'd love to talk.
